- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

35	def start_server():
36	    """Start the POLARIS live server in background."""
37	    log_file = SCREENSHOTS_DIR / "server.log"
38	    # Find a trace file for sample data
39	    trace_files = sorted(
40	        (PROJECT_ROOT / "logs").glob("pg_trace_*.jsonl"),
41	        key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime,
42	        reverse=True,
43	    )
44	    trace_arg = []
45	    if trace_files:
46	        trace_arg = ["--trace", str(trace_files[0])]
47	        print(f"  Using trace file: {trace_files[0].name}")
48	
49	    cmd = [
50	        sys.executable, str(PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"),
51	        "--port", str(PORT),
52	    ] + trace_arg
53	
54	    with open(log_file, "w") as f:
55	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
56	            cmd,
57	            stdout=f,
58	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
59	            cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
60	        )
61	    return proc

63

1	"""
2	Extract every env var referenced by live code under src/ + scripts/,
3	group by prefix, write a curated inventory to
4	docs/pipeline_audit_context/08_env_var_inventory.md.
5	"""
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import ast
9	import re
10	from collections import defaultdict
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
14	SRC = ROOT / "src"
15	SCRIPTS = ROOT / "scripts"
16	
17	# Match os.environ["X"], os.environ.get("X"), os.getenv("X")
18	ENV_RE = re.compile(
19	    r"""os\.(?:environ(?:\.get)?|getenv)\s*(?:\[|\()\s*["']([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*)["']"""
20	)
21	
22	
23	def extract_vars() -> dict[str, list[tuple[Path, int]]]:
24	    """Return {var_name: [(file_path, line_number), ...]}"""
25	    hits: dict[str, list[tuple[Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)
26	    for base in (SRC, SCRIPTS):
27	        for py in base.rglob("*.py"):
28	            if "__pycache__" in py.parts:
29	                continue
30	            try:
31	                lines = py.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines()
32	            except OSError:
33	                continue
34	            for ln_idx, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
35	                for m in ENV_RE.finditer(line):
36	                    hits[m.group(1)].append((py, ln_idx))
37	    return dict(hits)
38	
39	
40	def group_by_prefix(vars_: dict[str, list]) -> dict[str, dict[str, list]]:
41	    groups: dict[str, dict[str, list]] = defaultdict(dict)
42	    for name, uses in sorted(vars_.items()):
43	        if name.startswith("PG_"):
44	            prefix = "PG_* (pipeline A and shared)"
45	        elif name.startswith("POLARIS_"):
46	            prefix = "POLARIS_* (pipeline B UI + deployment)"
47	        elif name.startswith("OPENROUTER_"):
48	            prefix = "OPENROUTER_* (LLM gateway)"
49	        elif name.startswith("OPENAI_"):
50	            prefix = "OPENAI_* (OpenAI SDK)"
51	        elif name.startswith("POLARIS_") or name.startswith("PW_"):
52	            prefix = "PW_* (Playwright visual tests)"
53	        elif name.startswith("VQA_"):
54	            prefix = "VQA_* (visual QA tests)"
55	        elif name.startswith("FIRECRAWL_"):
56	            prefix = "FIRECRAWL_* (Firecrawl API)"
57	        elif name.startswith("EXA_") or name.startswith("PG_EXA_"):
58	            prefix = "EXA_* (Exa search)"
59	        elif name.startswith("JINA_") or name.startswith("PG_JINA_"):
60	            prefix = "JINA_* (Jina Reader)"
61	        elif name.startswith("VLLM_") or name.startswith("OLLAMA_"):
62	            prefix = "Local LLM (sovereign mode)"
63	        elif name.endswith("_API_KEY") or "KEY" in name:
64	            prefix = "API KEYS / secrets"
65	        else:
66	            prefix = "OTHER"
67	        groups[prefix][name] = uses
68	    return groups
69	
70	
71	def write_inventory():
72	    vars_ = extract_vars()
73	    groups = group_by_prefix(vars_)
74	    total = sum(len(g) for g in groups.values())
75	    out = ROOT / "docs" / "pipeline_audit_context" / "08_env_var_inventory.md"
76	    with open(out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
77	        f.write("# Environment variable inventory\n\n")
78	        f.write(
79	            f"Auto-generated from a static AST scan of `src/` + `scripts/` "
80	            f"on 2026-04-18. **{total} total env vars referenced** across "
81	            f"{len(list(SRC.rglob('*.py'))) + len(list(SCRIPTS.rglob('*.py')))} "
82	            f".py files. Produced by `scripts/produce_env_var_inventory.py`.\n\n"
83	        )
84	        f.write(
85	            "**Scope**: any `os.getenv(...)`, `os.environ[...]`, "
86	            "`os.environ.get(...)` with a literal string name. "
87	            "Dynamic env-var construction (f-strings, concatenation) is "
88	            "not captured here.\n\n"
89	        )
90	        f.write("## Summary by prefix\n\n")
91	        f.write("| Prefix | Count |\n|---|---|\n")
92	        for prefix in sorted(groups.keys()):
93	            f.write(f"| {prefix} | {len(groups[prefix])} |\n")
94	        f.write("\n---\n\n")
95	
96	        for prefix in sorted(groups.keys()):
97	            f.write(f"## {prefix}\n\n")
98	            f.write("| Variable | First referenced at |\n|---|---|\n")
99	            for name, uses in sorted(groups[prefix].items()):
100	                first = uses[0]
101	                rel = first[0].relative_to(ROOT).as_posix()
102	                f.write(f"| `{name}` | `{rel}:{first[1]}` |\n")
103	            f.write("\n")
104	
105	        f.write("---\n\n## Caveats\n\n")
106	        f.write(
107	            "- Only literal-string env vars are captured. "
108	            "`os.getenv(f\"PG_{x}_TIMEOUT\")` is invisible.\n"
109	            "- A variable referenced in multiple files shows only the "
110	            "FIRST occurrence. For full usage map, regenerate with a "
111	            "more detailed collector.\n"
112	            "- Pipeline membership (A vs B) is inferred from file path. "
113	            "Variables referenced in files shared across pipelines "
114	            "appear under whichever pipeline's file they're found first.\n"
115	        )
116	    print(f"Wrote {out}")
117	    print(f"Total vars: {total}")
118	    return total
119	
120	
121	if __name__ == "__main__":
122	    write_inventory()
123

1	"""Provision substrate for Phase 0 Task 0.3 — Vast.ai US dev cluster.
2	
3	This is a SUBSTRATE script, not a runtime. It does NOT call the Vast.ai
4	API. Per `feedback_substrate_is_not_product.md`, agent-actionable substrate
5	ships now; runtime provisioning waits for user authorization (cost +
6	account credentials).
7	
8	What this script does:
9	  - Loads a config (`config/provisioning/vast_dev_cluster.yaml`)
10	  - Validates required fields (gpu_type, region, max_hourly_usd, min_vram_gb)
11	  - Prints the dry-run plan (what would be requested)
12	  - In `--apply` mode, fail-loudly if VAST_API_KEY is missing
13	
14	When the user is ready to provision:
15	  1. Set VAST_API_KEY in .env
16	  2. Adjust config/provisioning/vast_dev_cluster.yaml
17	  3. Run `python scripts/provision_vast_dev_cluster.py --dry-run` to confirm
18	  4. Run `python scripts/provision_vast_dev_cluster.py --apply` (NOT YET
19	     IMPLEMENTED — substrate-only; needs vastai_sdk integration once
20	     hardware path A/B/C decision (Task 0.6) is locked)
21	
22	Per CLAUDE.md LAW II — no fake working: this script REFUSES to silently
23	provision; --apply is gated behind a NotImplementedError until the user
24	authorizes the integration.
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import argparse
30	import os
31	import sys
32	from dataclasses import dataclass
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	import yaml
36	
37	
38	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
39	_DEFAULT_CONFIG = _REPO_ROOT / "config" / "provisioning" / "vast_dev_cluster.yaml"
40	
41	
42	@dataclass(frozen=True)
43	class VastDevClusterConfig:
44	    gpu_type: str
45	    region: str
46	    max_hourly_usd: float
47	    min_vram_gb: int
48	    image: str
49	    disk_gb: int
50	    notes: str
51	
52	    @classmethod
53	    def from_yaml(cls, raw: dict) -> "VastDevClusterConfig":
54	        required = {
55	            "gpu_type", "region", "max_hourly_usd",
56	            "min_vram_gb", "image", "disk_gb",
57	        }
58	        missing = required - raw.keys()
59	        if missing:
60	            raise ValueError(f"missing required keys: {sorted(missing)}")
61	        if not isinstance(raw["max_hourly_usd"], (int, float)):
62	            raise ValueError("max_hourly_usd must be numeric")
63	        if raw["max_hourly_usd"] <= 0:
64	            raise ValueError("max_hourly_usd must be > 0")
65	        if not isinstance(raw["min_vram_gb"], int) or raw["min_vram_gb"] <= 0:
66	            raise ValueError("min_vram_gb must be positive int")
67	        if not isinstance(raw["disk_gb"], int) or raw["disk_gb"] <= 0:
68	            raise ValueError("disk_gb must be positive int")
69	        return cls(
70	            gpu_type=str(raw["gpu_type"]),
71	            region=str(raw["region"]),
72	            max_hourly_usd=float(raw["max_hourly_usd"]),
73	            min_vram_gb=int(raw["min_vram_gb"]),
74	            image=str(raw["image"]),
75	            disk_gb=int(raw["disk_gb"]),
76	            notes=str(raw.get("notes", "")),
77	        )
78	
79	
80	def load_config(path: Path) -> VastDevClusterConfig:
81	    if not path.is_file():
82	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"config not found: {path}")
83	    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
84	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
85	        raise ValueError(f"config must be a YAML mapping, got {type(raw).__name__}")
86	    return VastDevClusterConfig.from_yaml(raw)
87	
88	
89	def render_plan(config: VastDevClusterConfig) -> str:
90	    return (
91	        f"Vast.ai dev cluster provisioning plan:\n"
92	        f"  gpu_type:        {config.gpu_type}\n"
93	        f"  region:          {config.region}\n"
94	        f"  min_vram_gb:     {config.min_vram_gb}\n"
95	        f"  max_hourly_usd:  ${config.max_hourly_usd:.2f}\n"
96	        f"  image:           {config.image}\n"
97	        f"  disk_gb:         {config.disk_gb}\n"
98	        f"  notes:           {config.notes or '(none)'}"
99	    )
100	
101	
102	def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
103	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
104	        description="Provision substrate for Vast.ai US dev cluster"
105	    )
106	    p.add_argument(
107	        "--config",
108	        type=Path,
109	        default=_DEFAULT_CONFIG,
110	        help=f"Provisioning config (default: {_DEFAULT_CONFIG.relative_to(_REPO_ROOT)})",
111	    )
112	    g = p.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
113	    g.add_argument(
114	        "--dry-run", action="store_true",
115	        help="Validate config + print plan (default)",
116	    )
117	    g.add_argument(
118	        "--apply", action="store_true",
119	        help="Actually provision (NOT IMPLEMENTED — gated on Task 0.6 + VAST_API_KEY)",
120	    )
121	    return p.parse_args(argv)
122	
123	
124	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
125	    args = parse_args(argv)
126	    try:
127	        config = load_config(args.config)
128	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e:
129	        print(f"ERROR: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
130	        return 2
131	    plan = render_plan(config)
132	    print(plan)
133	    if args.apply:
134	        if not os.environ.get("VAST_API_KEY", "").strip():
135	            print(
136	                "ERROR: --apply requires VAST_API_KEY in env (LAW II — no fake working)",
137	                file=sys.stderr,
138	            )
139	            return 3
140	        raise NotImplementedError(
141	            "Vast.ai apply path is substrate-only. Phase 0 Task 0.6 "
142	            "(hardware Path A/B/C decision) must lock first. Track at "
143	            "polaris-controls/PLAN.md and the project task list."
144	        )
145	    print("\n(dry-run; no API calls made)")
146	    return 0
147	
148	
149	if __name__ == "__main__":
150	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Repair corpus metadata: populate DOI + journal/publisher + re-tier from signals we ALREADY have
3	(the direct_quote text, the source_url, the title). Deterministic, conservative, non-destructive.
4	
5	The bug this fixes: 992/995 rows had empty doi/journal and 253 were tier=UNKNOWN, even though the
6	source is clearly a peer-reviewed journal (e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo JEP, DOI in the text). Good sources
7	were being mislabeled, which (a) makes the corpus look low-quality and (b) tanks instruction-following
8	on benchmarks that demand journal sources. We recover the metadata instead of re-fetching.
9	
10	Rule of law (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): weight-and-consolidate, never filter. This only RE-LABELS tier and
11	FILLS missing metadata from evidence already present; it deletes nothing.
12	"""
13	import json, re, sys, collections, copy
14	
15	SRC = "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json"
16	OUT = "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.repaired.json"
17	
18	DOI_RE = re.compile(r'10\.\d{4,9}/[-._;()/:A-Za-z0-9]+')
19	
20	# Defensible domain -> (tier, publisher/venue-kind). Tiers per the project's T1(top primary) .. T7 scale.
21	# T1-T2 = peer-reviewed journals / top working-paper series; T3-T4 = gov/central-bank/IGO/institutional;
22	# T5 = industry/consulting; T6-T7 = tertiary/blog/marketing. Only assign on a CLEAR domain signal.
23	DOMAIN_TIER = {
24	    # peer-reviewed journals & top academic (T1)
25	    'aeaweb.org':'T1','sciencedirect.com':'T1','link.springer.com':'T1','cambridge.org':'T1',
26	    'emerald.com':'T1','nber.org':'T1','onlinelibrary.wiley.com':'T1','tandfonline.com':'T1',
27	    'journals.sagepub.com':'T1','academic.oup.com':'T1','mdpi.com':'T2','arxiv.org':'T2',
28	    'papers.ssrn.com':'T2','ideas.repec.org':'T2','econstor.eu':'T2','cepr.org':'T2',
29	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'T1','pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'T1','revistes.ub.edu':'T1',
30	    'econbiz.de':'T2','siepr.stanford.edu':'T2','economics.mit.edu':'T2','nature.com':'T1',
31	    # gov / central bank / IGO / institutional (T3-T4)
32	    'bls.gov':'T3','congress.gov':'T3','oecd.org':'T3','imf.org':'T3','ilo.org':'T3',
33	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org':'T3','newyorkfed.org':'T3','federalreserve.gov':'T3',
34	    'brookings.edu':'T4','reports.weforum.org':'T4','weforum.org':'T4','whitehouse.gov':'T3',
35	    # industry / consulting (T5)
36	    'goldmansachs.com':'T5','mckinsey.com':'T5','hbsp.harvard.edu':'T5','shrm-res.cloudinary.com':'T5',
37	    # tertiary / blog / marketing (T6-T7)
38	    'en.wikipedia.org':'T7','static1.squarespace.com':'T6','ssir.org':'T5','medium.com':'T7',
39	}
40	# publisher/venue label by domain (for the journal field when the content doesn't name it)
41	DOMAIN_VENUE = {
42	    'aeaweb.org':'American Economic Association','nber.org':'NBER Working Paper',
43	    'papers.ssrn.com':'SSRN','arxiv.org':'arXiv','ideas.repec.org':'RePEc','econstor.eu':'EconStor',
44	    'cepr.org':'CEPR','congress.gov':'Congressional Research Service','oecd.org':'OECD',
45	    'imf.org':'IMF','ilo.org':'ILO','bls.gov':'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics',
46	    'openknowledge.worldbank.org':'World Bank','newyorkfed.org':'Federal Reserve Bank of New York',
47	    'reports.weforum.org':'World Economic Forum','weforum.org':'World Economic Forum',
48	    'pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov':'PubMed Central','brookings.edu':'Brookings Institution',
49	}
50	# journal names commonly present in econ/AI-labor text -> use if found in the quote
51	JOURNAL_PAT = re.compile(r'(Journal of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Review of [A-Z][A-Za-z ]+|Quarterly Journal of [A-Za-z ]+|'
52	                         r'American Economic Review|Econometrica|Research Policy|Labour Economics|'
53	                         r'Technological Forecasting and Social Change|Nature[A-Za-z ]*)')
54	
55	def dom_of(url):
56	    p = url.split('/')
57	    return p[2].replace('www.','') if len(p) > 2 and url.startswith('http') else ''
58	
59	def repair_row(r, stats):
60	    url = str(r.get('source_url') or r.get('url') or '')
61	    quote = str(r.get('direct_quote','')); title = str(r.get('title',''))
62	    dom = dom_of(url)
63	    # 1) DOI
64	    if not str(r.get('doi','')).strip():
65	        m = DOI_RE.search(quote) or DOI_RE.search(url)
66	        if m: r['doi'] = m.group(0).rstrip('.'); stats['doi_filled'] += 1
67	    # 2) journal/venue
68	    if not str(r.get('journal','')).strip():
69	        jm = JOURNAL_PAT.search(quote) or JOURNAL_PAT.search(title)
70	        if jm: r['journal'] = jm.group(1).strip(); stats['journal_filled'] += 1
71	        elif dom in DOMAIN_VENUE: r['journal'] = DOMAIN_VENUE[dom]; stats['journal_filled'] += 1
72	    # 3) tier — only upgrade from UNKNOWN/empty on a CLEAR signal; never downgrade a real tier
73	    cur = str(r.get('tier') or '').strip()
74	    if cur in ('', 'UNKNOWN'):
75	        new = None
76	        if str(r.get('doi','')).strip(): new = DOMAIN_TIER.get(dom, 'T2')  # has DOI => at least a paper
77	        elif dom in DOMAIN_TIER: new = DOMAIN_TIER[dom]
78	        if new:
79	            r['tier'] = new; r['tier_repaired'] = True; stats['tier_reclassed'] += 1
80	            stats['tier_to'][new] += 1
81	    return r
82	
83	def main():
84	    d = json.load(open(SRC))
85	    rows = []
86	    def walk(o):
87	        if isinstance(o, dict):
88	            if o.get('direct_quote') is not None and (o.get('source_url') or o.get('url')): rows.append(o)
89	            for v in o.values(): walk(v)
90	        elif isinstance(o, list):
91	            for x in o: walk(x)
92	    walk(d)
93	
94	    before = collections.Counter(str(r.get('tier') or 'UNKNOWN') for r in rows)
95	    stats = {'doi_filled':0,'journal_filled':0,'tier_reclassed':0,'tier_to':collections.Counter()}
96	    for r in rows: repair_row(r, stats)
97	    after = collections.Counter(str(r.get('tier') or 'UNKNOWN') for r in rows)
98	
99	    # dedup detection (report only — do NOT delete, per §-1.3)
100	    key = collections.Counter()
101	    for r in rows:
102	        k = (str(r.get('doi','')).strip() or str(r.get('source_url') or r.get('url') or '') or str(r.get('title',''))).lower()
103	        key[k] += 1
104	    dups = sum(c-1 for c in key.values() if c > 1)
105	
106	    json.dump(d, open(OUT,'w'), indent=1)
107	    print("=== METADATA REPAIR ===")
108	    print(f"rows: {len(rows)}")
109	    print(f"DOI filled:        {stats['doi_filled']}")
110	    print(f"journal filled:    {stats['journal_filled']}")
111	    print(f"tier reclassified: {stats['tier_reclassed']}  -> {dict(stats['tier_to'])}")
112	    print(f"\nUNKNOWN before: {before.get('UNKNOWN',0)}   after: {after.get('UNKNOWN',0)}")
113	    print(f"tier dist AFTER: {dict(sorted(after.items()))}")
114	    print(f"\nduplicate rows detected (same doi/url/title): {dups} (reported, NOT deleted)")
115	    print(f"\nwrote repaired corpus -> {OUT}")
116	
117	if __name__ == '__main__':
118	    main()

1	"""
2	Playwright-based comprehensive visual audit of the POLARIS live dashboard.
3	
4	Launches the live server with injected test trace data, screenshots every tab
5	at multiple resolutions, captures key components, and runs 40 automated visual
6	checks covering layout, overview, queries, sources, STORM, evidence, graph,
7	report, and the right evidence panel.
8	
9	Outputs:
10	    - Screenshots:    outputs/visual_overhaul/{round}/  (PNG per tab + component)
11	    - JSON report:    outputs/visual_overhaul/audit_report.json
12	    - Console:        Summary table of 40 checks with PASS/FAIL
13	
14	Usage:
15	    python scripts/playwright_visual_overhaul.py
16	    python scripts/playwright_visual_overhaul.py --port 8765 --timeout 30
17	
18	Zero POLARIS src imports. Uses subprocess for server lifecycle management.
19	"""
20	
21	import argparse
22	import json
23	import os
24	import signal
25	import subprocess
26	import sys
27	import time
28	from datetime import datetime, timezone
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Optional
31	
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Configuration (LAW VI)
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	DEFAULT_PORT = int(os.getenv("PG_VISUAL_AUDIT_PORT", "8765"))
37	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_VISUAL_AUDIT_TIMEOUT", "30"))
38	INJECT_SCRIPT = "scripts/inject_test_trace.py"
39	SERVER_SCRIPT = "scripts/live_server.py"
40	TRACE_FILE = "logs/pg_trace_DASHBOARD_TEST.jsonl"
41	OUTPUT_DIR = Path("outputs/visual_overhaul")
42	DASHBOARD_URL_TEMPLATE = "http://localhost:{port}/"
43	
44	# Resolutions for responsive testing
45	RESOLUTIONS = {
46	    "desktop": {"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
47	    "tablet_landscape": {"width": 1024, "height": 768},
48	    "tablet_portrait": {"width": 768, "height": 1024},
49	}
50	
51	# All 9 tab identifiers matching data-tab attributes in the dashboard
52	TABS = [
53	    "overview", "queries", "sources", "storm",
54	    "evidence", "evgraph", "report", "trace", "fullreport",
55	]
56	
57	
58	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	# Check result accumulator
60	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	class AuditResult:
62	    """Accumulates check results and produces a structured report."""
63	
64	    def __init__(self):
65	        self.checks = []
66	        self.screenshots = []
67	        self.start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
68	
69	    def add_check(
70	        self,
71	        check_num: int,
72	        name: str,
73	        passed: bool,
74	        detail: str = "",
75	        category: str = "",
76	    ) -> None:
77	        status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
78	        icon = "[+]" if passed else "[-]"
79	        self.checks.append({
80	            "check": check_num,
81	            "name": name,
82	            "category": category,
83	            "passed": passed,
84	            "detail": detail,
85	        })
86	        print(f"  {icon} Check {check_num:2d}: {name} -- {status}"
87	              + (f" ({detail})" if detail else ""))
88	
89	    def add_screenshot(self, path: str, description: str) -> None:
90	        self.screenshots.append({"path": path, "description": description})
91	
92	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
93	        end_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
94	        passed = sum(1 for c in self.checks if c["passed"])
95	        failed = sum(1 for c in self.checks if not c["passed"])
96	        return {
97	            "audit_tool": "playwright_visual_overhaul",
98	            "start_time": self.start_time.isoformat(),
99	            "end_time": end_time.isoformat(),
100	            "duration_seconds": (end_time - self.start_time).total_seconds(),
101	            "total_checks": len(self.checks),
102	            "passed": passed,
103	            "failed": failed,
104	            "pass_rate": round(passed / max(len(self.checks), 1) * 100, 1),
105	            "checks": self.checks,
106	            "screenshots": self.screenshots,
107	        }
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Server lifecycle helpers
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	def inject_test_trace() -> bool:
114	    """Run inject_test_trace.py to generate synthetic JSONL trace data."""
115	    print("[1/4] Injecting test trace data...")
116	    result = subprocess.run(
117	        [sys.executable, INJECT_SCRIPT],
118	        capture_output=True,

120	        timeout=30,
121	    )
122	    if result.returncode != 0:
123	        print(f"  ERROR: inject_test_trace.py failed:\n{result.stderr}")
124	        return False
125	    print(f"  OK: {result.stdout.strip().splitlines()[0] if result.stdout.strip() else 'done'}")
126	    return True

128	
129	def kill_existing_server(port: int) -> None:
130	    """Kill any existing process on the given port (Windows-specific)."""
131	    try:
132	        result = subprocess.run(
133	            ["powershell.exe", "-NoProfile", "-Command",
134	             f"Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort {port} -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | "
135	             f"ForEach-Object {{ Stop-Process -Id $_.OwningProcess -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }}"],
136	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10,
137	        )
138	        if result.returncode == 0:
139	            print(f"  Killed existing process on port {port}")
140	    except Exception:
141	        pass

144	def start_server(port: int) -> subprocess.Popen:
145	    """Launch live_server.py as a background subprocess."""
146	    kill_existing_server(port)
147	    print(f"[2/4] Starting live server on port {port}...")
148	    proc = subprocess.Popen(
149	        [
150	            sys.executable, SERVER_SCRIPT,

152	            "--port", str(port),

154	        ],
155	        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,

158	        creationflags=getattr(subprocess, "CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP", 0),
159	    )
160	    return proc
161	
162	
163	def stop_server(proc: subprocess.Popen) -> None:
164	    """Gracefully stop the server subprocess."""
165	    if proc is None:
166	        return
167	    print("\n[CLEANUP] Stopping live server...")
168	    try:
169	        proc.terminate()
170	        try:
171	            proc.wait(timeout=5)
172	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
173	            proc.kill()
174	            proc.wait(timeout=3)
175	    except Exception as exc:
176	        print(f"  Warning: cleanup error: {exc}")
177	    print("  Server stopped.")

179	
180	def wait_for_server(url: str, timeout: int) -> bool:
181	    """Poll the server until it responds or timeout expires."""
182	    import urllib.request
183	    import urllib.error
184	
185	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
186	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
187	        try:
188	            resp = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=2)
189	            if resp.status == 200:
190	                print(f"  Server ready at {url}")
191	                return True
192	        except (urllib.error.URLError, ConnectionRefusedError, OSError):
193	            pass
194	        time.sleep(0.5)
195	    print(f"  ERROR: Server did not respond within {timeout}s")
196	    return False

1	"""M-34 re-gate: re-run PRISMA rule checks + evaluator_gate on an
2	existing sweep artifact directory WITHOUT re-generation or re-sweep.
3	
4	Use case: V23 full-scale aborted on PT11 (3/36 uncited decimals from
5	long-sentence window bug). M-34 fixes the bug in
6	`run_rule_checks`. This script replays the evaluator on the existing
7	report.md, recomputes the gate, and rewrites `manifest.json`,
8	`evaluator_rule_checks.json`, and the matching entry in
9	`sweep_summary.json`/`sweep_summary.md`.
10	
11	This is evaluator-only. It does NOT re-run the judge (already ran
12	during the original sweep; the judge verdicts stored in
13	`judge_output.json` (or legacy `qwen_judge_output.json`) are reused
14	verbatim).
15	
16	Usage:
17	    python scripts/regate_v23.py \\
18	        --sweep-root outputs/full_scale_v23 \\
19	        --slug clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import json
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from dataclasses import dataclass
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any
30	
31	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
32	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
33	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
34	
35	from src.polaris_graph.evaluator.evaluator_gate import compute_evaluator_gate
36	from src.polaris_graph.evaluator.external_evaluator import (
37	    RuleCheckResult,
38	    run_rule_checks,
39	)
40	
41	
42	@dataclass
43	class _EvOutShim:
44	    """Minimal shim of EvaluatorOutput for compute_evaluator_gate."""
45	    rule_checks: list[RuleCheckResult]
46	    contradictions_missing: list[str]
47	
48	
49	@dataclass
50	class _JudgeShim:
51	    """Minimal shim of JudgeResult for compute_evaluator_gate."""
52	    parse_ok: bool
53	    verdicts: dict[str, Any]
54	
55	
56	def _load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
57	    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
58	
59	
60	def _dump_json(path: Path, obj: Any) -> None:
61	    path.write_text(
62	        json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n",
63	        encoding="utf-8",
64	    )
65	
66	
67	def _status_from_gate(gate) -> str:
68	    """Mirror the orchestrator's status-selection logic for the
69	    evaluator-gated path. See run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1326-1344."""
70	    if gate.gate_class == "abort":
71	        return "abort_evaluator_critical"
72	    if gate.gate_class == "partial" and gate.judge_critical_axes:
73	        return "partial_evaluator_advisory"
74	    # I-run11-009 (#1055): a release-withholding gate (e.g. judge_parse_failed ->
75	    # advisory_unavailable + release_allowed=False) must NOT map to "success" — mirror the
76	    # fail-closed orchestrator ladder so the regate manifest cannot contradict the gate.
77	    if not getattr(gate, "release_allowed", True):
78	        return "abort_evaluator_critical"
79	    return "success"
80	
81	
82	def regate_run(run_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    report_path = run_dir / "report.md"
84	    manifest_path = run_dir / "manifest.json"
85	    rule_checks_path = run_dir / "evaluator_rule_checks.json"
86	    judge_path = run_dir / "judge_output.json"
87	    if not judge_path.exists():
88	        # I-modref-004 (#530): legacy V23 artifacts use the old filename.
89	        judge_path = run_dir / "qwen_judge_output.json"
90	    protocol_path = run_dir / "protocol.json"
91	    contradictions_path = run_dir / "contradictions.json"
92	    bibliography_path = run_dir / "bibliography.json"
93	
94	    report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
95	    manifest_prev = _load_json(manifest_path)
96	    rule_checks_prev = _load_json(rule_checks_path)
97	    judge_prev = _load_json(judge_path) if judge_path.exists() else {}
98	    protocol = _load_json(protocol_path)
99	    contradictions = (
100	        _load_json(contradictions_path)
101	        if contradictions_path.exists()
102	        else []
103	    )
104	    # evaluator_rule_checks.json has shape {... "rule_checks": [...], ...}
105	    contradictions_for_checks = (
106	        contradictions.get("contradictions", contradictions)
107	        if isinstance(contradictions, dict)
108	        else contradictions
109	    )
110	    biblio = _load_json(bibliography_path) if bibliography_path.exists() else []
111	    evidence_pool = {
112	        entry.get("evidence_id", f"ev_{i:03d}"): entry
113	        for i, entry in enumerate(biblio if isinstance(biblio, list) else [])
114	    }
115	
116	    generator_model = manifest_prev.get("generator", {}).get(
117	        "model",
118	        rule_checks_prev.get("generator_model", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
119	    )
120	    evaluator_model = rule_checks_prev.get(
121	        "evaluator_model", "google/gemma-4-31b-it"
122	    )
123	
124	    # I-deepfix-001 B4 P2-B (#1344): thread the generator/evaluator training families (derived from
125	    # the resolved model names) so PT03 verifies the two-family invariant HONESTLY on replay. Without
126	    # them, run_rule_checks now treats segregation as NOT-proven (conservative) for an LLM evaluator.
127	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import family_from_model  # noqa: PLC0415
128	    generator_family = family_from_model(generator_model)
129	    evaluator_family = family_from_model(evaluator_model)
130	
131	    # Reconstruct tier_distribution_report from manifest.corpus.tier_fractions.
132	    # The original sweep passes asdict(compute_tier_distribution(...)); we
133	    # only need the tier_fractions key for PT07's static check.
134	    corpus_block = manifest_prev.get("corpus", {}) or {}
135	    tier_distribution_report = None
136	    if "tier_fractions" in corpus_block:
137	        tier_distribution_report = {"tier_fractions": corpus_block["tier_fractions"]}
138	
139	    new_results, n_contra_disclosed, missing_contra = run_rule_checks(
140	        report_text=report_text,
141	        protocol=protocol,
142	        tier_distribution_report=tier_distribution_report,
143	        contradictions=contradictions_for_checks,
144	        evidence_pool=evidence_pool,
145	        generator_model=generator_model,
146	        evaluator_model=evaluator_model,
147	        generator_family=generator_family,
148	        evaluator_family=evaluator_family,
149	    )
150	
151	    new_rule_checks_payload = dict(rule_checks_prev)
152	    new_rule_checks_payload["rule_checks"] = [
153	        {
154	            "item_id": r.item_id,
155	            "name": r.name,
156	            "passed": r.passed,
157	            "details": r.details,
158	        }
159	        for r in new_results
160	    ]
161	    new_rule_checks_payload["contradictions_disclosed"] = n_contra_disclosed
162	    new_rule_checks_payload["contradictions_missing"] = missing_contra
163	    new_rule_checks_payload["_regate_note"] = (
164	        "Re-gated under M-34 (PT11 lookahead widen). Prior gate was "
165	        f"status={manifest_prev.get('status')!r}, "
166	        f"release_allowed={manifest_prev.get('release_allowed')}."
167	    )
168	
169	    ev_shim = _EvOutShim(
170	        rule_checks=new_results,
171	        contradictions_missing=missing_contra,
172	    )
173	    judge_verdicts = judge_prev.get("verdicts", {}) if isinstance(judge_prev, dict) else {}
174	    judge_shim = _JudgeShim(
175	        parse_ok=bool(judge_verdicts),
176	        verdicts=judge_verdicts,
177	    )
178	    adequacy = manifest_prev.get("adequacy")
179	    completeness = manifest_prev.get("completeness")
180	    new_gate = compute_evaluator_gate(
181	        ev_shim,
182	        judge_result=judge_shim,
183	        adequacy=adequacy,
184	        completeness=completeness,
185	    )
186	    new_status = _status_from_gate(new_gate)
187	
188	    new_manifest = dict(manifest_prev)
189	    new_manifest["evaluator_gate"] = new_gate.to_dict()
190	    new_manifest["release_allowed"] = new_gate.release_allowed
191	    new_manifest["evaluator_rule_pass"] = sum(
192	        1 for r in new_results if r.passed
193	    )
194	    new_manifest["evaluator_rule_fail"] = sum(
195	        1 for r in new_results if not r.passed
196	    )
197	    new_manifest["status"] = new_status
198	    new_manifest["_regate_prev_status"] = manifest_prev.get("status")
199	    new_manifest["_regate_prev_release_allowed"] = manifest_prev.get(
200	        "release_allowed"
201	    )
202	    new_manifest["_regate_note"] = (
203	        "Re-gated under M-34 (commit bf78396) — PT11 lookahead-window "
204	        "widened 200→1000 chars. No re-generation or re-retrieval."
205	    )
206	
207	    _dump_json(manifest_path, new_manifest)
208	    _dump_json(rule_checks_path, new_rule_checks_payload)
209	
210	    return {
211	        "run_dir": str(run_dir),
212	        "prev_status": manifest_prev.get("status"),
213	        "prev_release_allowed": manifest_prev.get("release_allowed"),
214	        "new_status": new_status,
215	        "new_release_allowed": new_gate.release_allowed,
216	        "pt11_details": next(
217	            (r.details for r in new_results if r.item_id == "PT11"),
218	            "",
219	        ),
220	        "evaluator_rule_pass": new_manifest["evaluator_rule_pass"],
221	        "evaluator_rule_fail": new_manifest["evaluator_rule_fail"],
222	        "gate_reasons": new_gate.reasons,
223	    }
224	
225	
226	def update_sweep_summary(
227	    sweep_root: Path,
228	    slug: str,
229	    regate_result: dict[str, Any],
230	) -> None:
231	    summary_path = sweep_root / "sweep_summary.json"
232	    if not summary_path.exists():
233	        return
234	    summary = _load_json(summary_path)
235	    if not isinstance(summary, list):
236	        return
237	    for entry in summary:
238	        if entry.get("slug") == slug:
239	            entry["status"] = regate_result["new_status"]
240	            manifest = entry.get("manifest")
241	            if isinstance(manifest, dict):
242	                manifest["status"] = regate_result["new_status"]
243	                manifest["release_allowed"] = regate_result["new_release_allowed"]
244	                manifest["evaluator_rule_pass"] = regate_result["evaluator_rule_pass"]
245	                manifest["evaluator_rule_fail"] = regate_result["evaluator_rule_fail"]
246	            entry["_regate_note"] = (
247	                f"Re-gated under M-34; prior status "
248	                f"{regate_result['prev_status']!r} "
249	                f"(release_allowed={regate_result['prev_release_allowed']})."
250	            )
251	            break
252	    _dump_json(summary_path, summary)
253	
254	    # Also patch the matrix markdown for operator visibility.
255	    md_path = sweep_root / "sweep_summary.md"
256	    if md_path.exists():
257	        md = md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
258	        md += (
259	            f"\n\n> Note: {slug} re-gated under M-34 on re-evaluation. "
260	            f"Prior status `{regate_result['prev_status']}` → "
261	            f"new status `{regate_result['new_status']}` "
262	            f"(release_allowed={regate_result['new_release_allowed']}).\n"
263	        )
264	        md_path.write_text(md, encoding="utf-8")
265	
266	
267	def main() -> int:
268	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
269	    parser.add_argument(
270	        "--sweep-root",
271	        default="outputs/full_scale_v23",
272	        help="Sweep output root directory (default: outputs/full_scale_v23)",
273	    )
274	    parser.add_argument(
275	        "--slug",
276	        default="clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm",
277	        help="Slug to re-gate (default: clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm)",
278	    )
279	    args = parser.parse_args()
280	
281	    sweep_root = Path(args.sweep_root)
282	    # Find the run directory matching the slug.
283	    candidates = list(sweep_root.rglob("manifest.json"))
284	    target = None
285	    for c in candidates:
286	        if c.parent.name == args.slug:
287	            target = c.parent
288	            break
289	    if target is None:
290	        print(f"ERROR: slug {args.slug!r} not found under {sweep_root}")
291	        return 2
292	    print(f"Re-gating {target}")
293	    result = regate_run(target)
294	    update_sweep_summary(sweep_root, args.slug, result)
295	    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
296	    return 0
297	
298	
299	if __name__ == "__main__":
300	    raise SystemExit(main())
301

1	"""Full-scale ReAct analysis test on REAL evidence from PG_TEST_047.
2	
3	Loads 258 evidence pieces about PFAS water filtration, runs the ReAct
4	agent with PG_REACT_MAX_ITERATIONS=5, and prints detailed diagnostics
5	showing every tool choice, reasoning, output quality, and citation chain.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    python -u scripts/react_full_scale_test.py
9	"""
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import json
13	import os
14	import re
15	import sys
16	import time
17	
18	
19	async def main():
20	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
21	    # 1. Load real evidence from PG_TEST_047
22	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
23	    result_path = "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_047.json"
24	    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
25	    print("POLARIS v3 ReAct Full-Scale Test")
26	    print(f"{'='*80}")
27	    print(f"Loading evidence from: {result_path}")
28	
29	    with open(result_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
30	        result_data = json.load(f)
31	
32	    raw_evidence = result_data.get("evidence", [])
33	    query = result_data.get("original_query", "")
34	    print(f"Query: {query}")
35	    print(f"Evidence pieces loaded: {len(raw_evidence)}")
36	
37	    # Build evidence_store dict (keyed by evidence_id)
38	    evidence_store = {}
39	    for ev in raw_evidence:
40	        eid = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
41	        if not eid:
42	            continue
43	        evidence_store[eid] = ev
44	
45	    evidence_ids = list(evidence_store.keys())
46	
47	    # Quick stats
48	    tiers = {}
49	    numeric_count = 0
50	    for ev in evidence_store.values():
51	        t = ev.get("quality_tier", "UNKNOWN")
52	        tiers[t] = tiers.get(t, 0) + 1
53	        stmt = ev.get("statement", "")
54	        if re.search(r'\d+\.?\d*\s*(%|mg|ppt|ppb|ppm|ng|mgd|\$)', stmt):
55	            numeric_count += 1
56	
57	    print(f"Tier distribution: {tiers}")
58	    print(f"Evidence with numeric data: {numeric_count}/{len(evidence_store)}")
59	
60	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
61	    # 2. Configure ReAct agent for full run
62	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
63	    os.environ["PG_REACT_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "5"
64	    os.environ["PG_REACT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "180"
65	    os.environ["PG_REACT_TOOL_TIMEOUT"] = "60"
66	
67	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
68	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.react_agent import ReactAnalysisAgent
69	
70	    client = OpenRouterClient(session_id="react_full_scale")
71	    print(f"\nLLM: {client.model}")
72	    print(f"Max iterations: 5, Timeout: 180s, Tool timeout: 60s")
73	
74	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
75	    # 3. Run the ReAct agent
76	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
77	    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
78	    print("RUNNING REACT AGENT...")
79	    print(f"{'='*80}\n")
80	
81	    start_time = time.monotonic()
82	
83	    agent = ReactAnalysisAgent(
84	        client=client,
85	        evidence_store=evidence_store,
86	        evidence_ids=evidence_ids,
87	        query=query,
88	    )
89	    notebook = await agent.run()
90	
91	    total_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
92	
93	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
94	    # 4. Print detailed diagnostics for every step
95	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
96	    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
97	    print("STEP-BY-STEP TRACE")
98	    print(f"{'='*80}")
99	
100	    for step in notebook.steps:
101	        status = "OK" if step.result.success else "FAIL"
102	        print(f"\n--- Step {step.step_number}: {step.tool_name} [{status}] "
103	              f"({step.elapsed_seconds:.2f}s) ---")
104	        print(f"  REASONING: {step.reasoning}")
105	
106	        if step.result.success:
107	            ev_ids = step.result.source_evidence_ids
108	            print(f"  Evidence IDs: {len(ev_ids)} unique "
109	                  f"(first 5: {ev_ids[:5]})")
110	            print(f"  Data points produced: {len(step.result.data_points_produced)}")
111	            print(f"  Charts: {len(step.result.charts)}")
112	            print(f"  Insights ({len(step.result.insights)}):")
113	            for insight in step.result.insights[:3]:
114	                print(f"    - {insight[:120]}")
115	            if step.result.statistics:
116	                print(f"  Statistics: {json.dumps(step.result.statistics, default=str)[:200]}")
117	
118	            # Show markdown preview
119	            md = step.result.markdown
120	            print(f"  Markdown ({len(md)} chars):")
121	            # Show first 500 chars
122	            for line in md[:500].split("\n"):
123	                print(f"    {line}")
124	            if len(md) > 500:
125	                print(f"    ... ({len(md) - 500} more chars)")
126	        else:
127	            print(f"  ERROR: {step.result.error}")
128	
129	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
130	    # 5. Citation provenance analysis
131	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
132	    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
133	    print("CITATION PROVENANCE ANALYSIS")
134	    print(f"{'='*80}")
135	
136	    context = notebook.build_synthesis_context()
137	    cite_tokens = re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]", context)
138	    unique_cited = set(cite_tokens)
139	
140	    print(f"Total [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens in context: {len(cite_tokens)}")
141	    print(f"Unique evidence IDs cited: {len(unique_cited)}")
142	    print(f"All source evidence IDs collected: "
143	          f"{len(notebook.get_all_source_evidence_ids())}")
144	
145	    # Verify all cited IDs exist in evidence_store
146	    phantom_cites = [c for c in unique_cited if c not in evidence_store]
147	    print(f"Phantom citations (ID not in evidence): {len(phantom_cites)}")
148	    if phantom_cites:
149	        print(f"  PHANTOM IDs: {phantom_cites[:10]}")
150	
151	    # Check for "POLARIS" leakage
152	    polaris_count = context.count("POLARIS")
153	    toolkit_count = context.count("Analysis Toolkit")
154	    print(f"'POLARIS' in context: {polaris_count}")
155	    print(f"'Analysis Toolkit' in context: {toolkit_count}")
156	
157	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
158	    # 6. AnalysisEntry output quality
159	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
160	    entries = notebook.to_entries()

1	"""Stress test: ReAct analysis on 5 diverse evidence sets with 6-axis audit.
2	
3	Loads REAL evidence from previous pipeline runs, runs the ReAct agent,
4	then audits every line of output using a 6-axis evaluation:
5	
6	Axis 1: Factual Accuracy (25 pts) — numerical claim verification
7	Axis 2: Synthesis Quality (25 pts) — parroting, cross-source integration
8	Axis 3: Question Answering (20 pts) — multi-criteria integration, ranking
9	Axis 4: Evidence Utilization (10 pts) — category coverage
10	Axis 5: Citation Quality (10 pts) — count, validity
11	Axis 6: Content Quality (10 pts) — density, units, zero leakage
12	
13	Plus line-by-line verification: full interpretation output printed,
14	5 random citations spot-checked per run against source evidence.
15	
16	Usage:
17	    python -u -m scripts.react_stress_test
18	"""
19	
20	import asyncio
21	import json
22	import math
23	import os
24	import random
25	import re
26	import sys
27	import time
28	from collections import Counter
29	from pathlib import Path

31	# Windows console encoding fix: LLM output contains Unicode chars that
32	# cp1252 can't encode. Force UTF-8 with replacement on error.
33	if sys.stdout.encoding and sys.stdout.encoding.lower() != "utf-8":
34	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
35	if sys.stderr.encoding and sys.stderr.encoding.lower() != "utf-8":
36	    sys.stderr.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

38	import numpy as np

41	
42	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	# WS-4: Statistical evaluation framework (Bayesian CIs, multi-run protocol)
44	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	
46	def bayesian_ci(scores: list[float], alpha: float = 0.05) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
47	    """Bayesian credible interval (Beta-Binomial) for [0,100] scores.
48	
49	    Returns (mean, lower_bound, upper_bound) as percentages.
50	    Uses uninformative Beta(1,1) prior. Per ICML 2025 recommendation,
51	    CLT/bootstrap CIs dramatically underestimate uncertainty for small N.
52	    """
53	    if not scores:
54	        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
55	    normalized = np.array(scores) / 100.0
56	    a_post = 1 + np.sum(normalized)
57	    b_post = 1 + len(normalized) - np.sum(normalized)
58	    lower = stats.beta.ppf(alpha / 2, a_post, b_post) * 100
59	    upper = stats.beta.ppf(1 - alpha / 2, a_post, b_post) * 100
60	    return float(np.mean(scores)), float(lower), float(upper)
61	
62	
63	def compare_configs(
64	    scores_a: list[float], scores_b: list[float],
65	) -> tuple[float, float]:
66	    """Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired runs.
67	
68	    Requires 10+ paired observations for reliable p-values.
69	    Returns (test_statistic, p_value).
70	    """
71	    if len(scores_a) < 6 or len(scores_b) < 6:
72	        return 0.0, 1.0  # Insufficient data
73	    if len(scores_a) != len(scores_b):
74	        n = min(len(scores_a), len(scores_b))
75	        scores_a = scores_a[:n]
76	        scores_b = scores_b[:n]
77	    stat, p = stats.wilcoxon(scores_a, scores_b)
78	    return float(stat), float(p)

113	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	# Deep content audit functions

116	
117	async def audit_citations(context: str, evidence_store: dict) -> dict:
118	    """Audit every [CITE:ev_xxx] token in the context.
119	
120	    Uses a LINE-level context window for list items (not 200-char window)
121	    to avoid cross-item bleed. A citation on a list item about "PFOS 34 ng/L"
122	    should be checked against THAT line, not the header 3 lines above.
123	
124	    WP-3.1: Made async to avoid `run_until_complete()` inside running loop.
125	    """
126	    cite_pattern = re.compile(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]')
127	    all_cites = cite_pattern.findall(context)
128	    unique_cites = set(all_cites)
129	
130	    phantom = [c for c in unique_cites if c not in evidence_store]
131	    valid = [c for c in unique_cites if c in evidence_store]
132	
133	    # Check if cited evidence is relevant to its immediate line
134	    mismatched = []
135	    lines = context.split("\n")
136	    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines):
137	        for match in cite_pattern.finditer(line):
138	            eid = match.group(1)
139	            if eid not in evidence_store:
140	                continue
141	
142	            # Use the LINE containing the citation as context
143	            # Plus the line above for additional context
144	            ctx_lines = []
145	            if line_num > 0:
146	                ctx_lines.append(lines[line_num - 1])
147	            ctx_lines.append(line)
148	            surrounding = " ".join(ctx_lines).lower()
149	
150	            # Get the evidence statement
151	            ev_stmt = evidence_store[eid].get("statement", "").lower()
152	
153	            # Extract content words (4+ chars, skip common words)
154	            surr_words = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', surrounding))
155	            ev_words = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ev_stmt))
156	            overlap = surr_words & ev_words
157	
158	            # Also check number overlap (shared numeric values)
159	            surr_nums = set(re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*', surrounding))
160	            ev_nums = set(re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*', ev_stmt))
161	            num_overlap = surr_nums & ev_nums
162	
163	            # Pass if: 2+ word overlap OR 1+ number overlap OR
164	            # citation is on a table/summary line (structural citation)
165	            is_structural = (
166	                line.strip().startswith("|")
167	                or line.strip().startswith("**Summary")
168	                or line.strip().startswith("**Overall")
169	                or "across" in line.lower()
170	            )
171	
172	            if len(overlap) < 2 and len(num_overlap) == 0 and not is_structural:
173	                mismatched.append({
174	                    "evidence_id": eid,
175	                    "context_line": line.strip()[:100],
176	                    "evidence_statement": ev_stmt[:100],
177	                    "word_overlap": len(overlap),
178	                    "number_overlap": len(num_overlap),
179	                })
180	
181	    # R2: MiniCheck NLI verification (WS-1 SOTA) with embedding fallback.
182	    # MiniCheck-FT5 matches GPT-4 factuality at 400x lower cost (EMNLP 2024).
183	    # Falls back to cosine similarity when MiniCheck is unavailable.
184	    if mismatched:
185	        minicheck_available = False
186	        try:
187	            if os.getenv("PG_NLI_ENABLED", "0") == "1":
188	                from src.polaris_graph.agents.nli_verifier import load_nli_model
189	                scorer = await load_nli_model()
190	                if scorer is not None:
191	                    minicheck_available = True
192	                    ctx_texts = [m["context_line"] for m in mismatched]
193	                    ev_texts = [m["evidence_statement"] for m in mismatched]
194	                    _labels, probs, _chunks, _chunk_probs = scorer.score(
195	                        docs=ev_texts, claims=ctx_texts,
196	                    )
197	                    rescued = []
198	                    still_mismatched = []
199	                    n = len(mismatched)
200	                    for i, m_item in enumerate(mismatched):
201	                        p = float(probs[i])
202	                        if p >= 0.25:  # MiniCheck entailment threshold
203	                            rescued.append(m_item["evidence_id"])
204	                        else:
205	                            still_mismatched.append(m_item)
206	                    if rescued:
207	                        print(f"    R2: MiniCheck rescued {len(rescued)}/{n} "
208	                              f"citations (NLI >= 0.25)")
209	                    mismatched = still_mismatched
210	        except (ImportError, Exception) as exc:
211	            print(f"    R2: MiniCheck unavailable ({type(exc).__name__}), "
212	                  f"falling back to embedding similarity")
213	
214	        # Embedding fallback when MiniCheck unavailable
215	        if not minicheck_available and mismatched:
216	            try:
217	                from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
218	                rescued = []
219	                still_mismatched = []
220	                ctx_texts = [m["context_line"] for m in mismatched]
221	                ev_texts = [m["evidence_statement"] for m in mismatched]
222	                all_texts = ctx_texts + ev_texts
223	                all_vecs = embed_texts(all_texts)
224	                n = len(mismatched)
225	                for i, m_item in enumerate(mismatched):
226	                    ctx_vec = all_vecs[i]
227	                    ev_vec = all_vecs[n + i]
228	                    dot = sum(a * b for a, b in zip(ctx_vec, ev_vec))
229	                    mag_a = sum(a * a for a in ctx_vec) ** 0.5
230	                    mag_b = sum(b * b for b in ev_vec) ** 0.5
231	                    sim = dot / (mag_a * mag_b) if mag_a > 0 and mag_b > 0 else 0.0
232	                    if sim >= 0.50:
233	                        rescued.append(m_item["evidence_id"])
234	                    else:
235	                        still_mismatched.append(m_item)
236	                if rescued:
237	                    print(f"    R2: Embedding rescued {len(rescued)}/{n} "
238	                          f"citations (sim >= 0.50)")
239	                mismatched = still_mismatched
240	            except ImportError:
241	                pass  # embedding_service not available — skip fallback
242	
243	    return {
244	        "total_cite_tokens": len(all_cites),
245	        "unique_cited": len(unique_cites),
246	        "phantom_citations": phantom,
247	        "valid_citations": len(valid),
248	        "mismatched_citations": mismatched,
249	        "citation_density": len(all_cites) / max(len(context.split()), 1) * 100,
250	    }
251	
252	
253	def audit_information_density(context: str) -> dict:
254	    """Measure how much of the text is substantive vs filler."""
255	    lines = [l.strip() for l in context.split("\n") if l.strip()]
256	
257	    filler_patterns = [
258	        r'^#+\s',           # Headers (not content)
259	        r'^[-*]\s*$',       # Empty list items
260	        r'^\|.*\|$',        # Table rows (counted separately)
261	        r'^---',            # Separators
262	        r'^\s*$',           # Blank
263	    ]
264	
265	    table_lines = 0
266	    header_lines = 0
267	    filler_lines = 0
268	    content_lines = 0
269	
270	    for line in lines:
271	        if re.match(r'^\|.*\|$', line):
272	            table_lines += 1
273	        elif re.match(r'^#+\s', line):
274	            header_lines += 1
275	        elif any(re.match(p, line) for p in filler_patterns):
276	            filler_lines += 1
277	        else:
278	            content_lines += 1
279	
280	    # R1: Expanded unit regex covering all scientific domains.
281	    # Word-boundary guards on ambiguous short units (m, N, Da).
282	    _unit_rx = (
283	        r'%|mg/[Ll]|µg/[Ll]|ng/[Ll]|g/L|mg/g|'        # concentration
284	        r'ppt|ppb|ppm|'                                   # parts-per
285	        r'kDa|Da\b|'                                      # molecular wt
286	        r'GPa|MPa|kPa|Pa\b|bar\b|atm\b|'                # pressure
287	        r'(?:µ|n|m|c|k)m\b|'                             # length (nm µm mm cm km)
288	        r'°[CF]|'                                         # temperature
289	        r'[Ll]/min|m[Ll]/min|m³/h|'                      # flow rate
290	        r'(?:k|m)?N\b|'                                   # force
291	        r'g/m2|kg/m2|m2\b|'                              # area / density
292	        r'k?Wh|MWh|'                                      # energy
293	        r'(?:m|k)?Hz|rpm|'                                # frequency
294	        r'\$|USD|EUR'                                      # currency
295	    )
296	    # Count numbers in content (sign of data density)
297	    numbers_found = len(re.findall(
298	        r'\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:' + _unit_rx + r')',
299	        context,
300	    ))
301	
302	    # Count unique factual claims (sentences with numbers + units)
303	    factual_sentences = len(re.findall(
304	        r'[A-Z][^.!?]*\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:' + _unit_rx + r')[^.!?]*[.!?]',
305	        context,
306	    ))
307	
308	    total = len(lines) or 1
309	    return {
310	        "total_lines": len(lines),
311	        "content_lines": content_lines,
312	        "table_lines": table_lines,
313	        "header_lines": header_lines,
314	        "filler_lines": filler_lines,
315	        "content_ratio": round(content_lines / total * 100, 1),
316	        "numbers_with_units": numbers_found,
317	        "factual_sentences": factual_sentences,
318	        "data_density": round(numbers_found / total * 100, 1),
319	    }
320	
321	
322	def audit_structured_artifacts(context: str) -> dict:
323	    """Count structured analytical artifacts in the output.
324	
325	    Detects comparison tables, conditional recommendations, executive
326	    summaries, cost calculations, and decision matrices.
327	    """
328	    # Count table rows (subtract header + separator lines)
329	    table_line_count = len(
330	        re.findall(r'^\|.*\|.*\|$', context, re.MULTILINE),
331	    )
332	    table_rows = max(0, table_line_count - 2)
333	
334	    # Conditional recommendations: "If X then Y" pattern
335	    conditionals = len(re.findall(
336	        r'\*\*[Ii]f\*\*.*\*\*then\*\*', context,
337	    ))
338	    # Also catch unbolded "If...then" patterns
339	    conditionals += len(re.findall(
340	        r'(?:^|\. )If\s+.{10,80}\s+then\s+', context, re.MULTILINE,
341	    ))
342	
343	    # Executive summary: first substantive paragraph (50-600 chars)
344	    # Skip headers (# or **HEADER**) to find actual prose
345	    first_paragraph = ""
346	    for para in context.split("\n\n"):
347	        stripped = para.strip()
348	        if not stripped:
349	            continue
350	        # Bold-only header line (entire paragraph is just a header)
351	        if re.match(r'^\*\*[A-Z ]+\*\*$', stripped):
352	            continue
353	        # Strip leading header/bold lines from the paragraph,
354	        # then check if substantive prose remains
355	        cleaned = stripped
356	        # Strip "# Header" line at start of paragraph
357	        cleaned = re.sub(
358	            r'^#{1,4}\s+[^\n]*\n?', '', cleaned,
359	        ).strip()
360	        # Strip "**BOLD HEADER**:" prefix on same line
361	        cleaned = re.sub(
362	            r'^\*\*[A-Z ]+\*\*:?\s*\n?', '', cleaned,
363	        ).strip()
364	        if cleaned and len(cleaned) >= 30:
365	            first_paragraph = cleaned
366	            break
367	    has_exec_summary = 50 <= len(first_paragraph) <= 600
368	
369	    # Cost calculations: dollar amounts with math operators
370	    cost_calcs = len(re.findall(
371	        r'\$[\d,.]+.*(?:per|/|x|\u00d7|=)', context, re.IGNORECASE,
372	    ))
373	
374	    # Decision matrices: tables with weight/score/total headers
375	    decision_matrices = len(re.findall(
376	        r'\|.*(?:weight|score|total).*\|', context, re.IGNORECASE,
377	    ))
378	
379	    return {
380	        "table_rows": table_rows,
381	        "conditional_recommendations": conditionals,
382	        "has_executive_summary": has_exec_summary,
383	        "cost_calculations": cost_calcs,
384	        "decision_matrices": decision_matrices,
385	    }
386	
387	
388	def audit_statistics(entries: list, data_points: list) -> dict:
389	    """Verify statistical claims against raw data using same unit grouping."""
390	    issues = []
391	    verified = 0
392	
393	    for entry_dict in entries:
394	        stats = entry_dict.get("statistics", {})
395	        if not stats or not stats.get("mean"):
396	            continue
397	
398	        claimed_mean = stats.get("mean")
399	        claimed_n = stats.get("n")
400	
401	        if not data_points or not claimed_n:
402	            continue
403	
404	        # Group by unit (same logic as _wrap_statistical_summary)
405	        by_unit: dict[str, list] = {}
406	        for dp in data_points:
407	            unit = dp.get("unit", "unknown") or "unknown"
408	            try:
409	                v = float(str(dp.get("value", "")).replace(",", ""))
410	                by_unit.setdefault(unit, []).append(v)
411	            except (ValueError, TypeError):
412	                pass
413	
414	        if not by_unit:
415	            continue
416	
417	        # Use the largest unit group (same as the tool does)
418	        primary_unit = max(by_unit, key=lambda u: len(by_unit[u]))
419	        primary_values = by_unit[primary_unit]
420	
421	        if not primary_values:
422	            continue
423	
424	        actual_mean = sum(primary_values) / len(primary_values)
425	
426	        # Verify: claimed mean should match unit-filtered mean
427	        if claimed_mean != 0:
428	            pct_diff = abs(actual_mean - claimed_mean) / abs(claimed_mean)
429	            if pct_diff > 0.01:  # >1% difference
430	                issues.append({
431	                    "type": "mean_mismatch",
432	                    "claimed": round(claimed_mean, 4),
433	                    "computed_unit_filtered": round(actual_mean, 4),
434	                    "unit": primary_unit,
435	                    "n_claimed": claimed_n,
436	                    "n_actual": len(primary_values),
437	                    "pct_diff": round(pct_diff * 100, 1),
438	                })
439	            else:
440	                verified += 1
441	        else:
442	            verified += 1
443	
444	    return {
445	        "entries_with_stats": sum(1 for e in entries if e.get("statistics", {}).get("mean")),
446	        "verified": verified,
447	        "verification_issues": issues,
448	    }
449	
450	
451	def audit_insights(entries: list) -> dict:
452	    """Check if insights are substantive or generic water."""
453	    generic_patterns = [
454	        r'^extracted \d+ numeric',       # Just restating the tool action
455	        r'^meta-analysis across',         # Generic structure
456	        r'^consensus strength:',          # Just repeating agreement label
457	    ]
458	
459	    total_insights = 0
460	    substantive = 0
461	    generic = 0
462	    unique_insights = set()
463	
464	    for entry_dict in entries:
465	        for insight in entry_dict.get("insights", []):
466	            total_insights += 1
467	            lower = insight.lower().strip()
468	            unique_insights.add(lower)
469	
470	            is_generic = any(re.match(p, lower) for p in generic_patterns)
471	            if is_generic:
472	                generic += 1
473	            else:
474	                # Substantive = contains a specific number + context
475	                if re.search(r'\d+\.?\d*', insight) and len(insight) > 30:
476	                    substantive += 1
477	                elif len(insight) > 50:
478	                    substantive += 1
479	                else:

733	    against any single evidence statement (using 4+ char words only).
734	    """
735	    sentences = [s.strip() for s in re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', context) if s.strip()]

752	            union = sent_words | ev_words

1015	    support_threshold = float(os.getenv(

1055	def audit_originality(context: str, evidence_store: dict) -> dict:
1056	    """Embedding-based originality detection.
1057	
1058	    Replaces Jaccard word-overlap parroting detector. Uses cosine
1059	    similarity between output sentences and evidence statements.
1060	    Only flags sentences with very high similarity (>0.92) AND
1061	    no analytical markers — technical terminology alone won't trigger.
1062	    """
1063	    from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
1064	
1065	    # Analytical markers that indicate synthesis (not copying)
1066	    analytical_markers = {
1067	        "however", "therefore", "trade-off", "tradeoff", "suggests",
1068	        "implies", "compared", "indicates", "whereas", "consequently",
1069	        "despite", "furthermore", "moreover", "notably", "in contrast",
1070	        "on the other hand", "this suggests", "this indicates",
1071	        "this creates", "this highlights", "this means", "ranking",
1072	        "rank", "trade-offs", "reveals", "necessitating",
1073	    }
1074	
1075	    # Clean citations from sentences before embedding
1076	    cite_re = re.compile(r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]')
1077	    sentences = [
1078	        s.strip() for s in re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', context) if len(s.strip()) > 20
1079	    ]
1080	    clean_sentences = [cite_re.sub('', s).strip() for s in sentences]
1081	
1082	    # Collect evidence statements
1083	    ev_statements = [
1084	        ev.get("statement", "")
1085	        for ev in evidence_store.values()
1086	        if ev.get("statement", "") and len(ev.get("statement", "")) > 10
1087	    ]
1088	
1089	    if not clean_sentences or not ev_statements:
1090	        return {"total_sentences": len(sentences), "original": len(sentences),
1091	                "copied": 0, "copy_ratio": 0.0, "examples": []}
1092	
1093	    try:
1094	        sent_embeddings = embed_texts(clean_sentences)
1095	        ev_embeddings = embed_texts(ev_statements)
1096	
1097	        sent_matrix = np.array(sent_embeddings)
1098	        ev_matrix = np.array(ev_embeddings)
1099	
1100	        # Cosine similarity (embeddings are L2-normalized)
1101	        sim_matrix = sent_matrix @ ev_matrix.T
1102	
1103	        copied = 0
1104	        examples = []
1105	        for i, sent in enumerate(sentences):
1106	            max_sim = float(np.max(sim_matrix[i]))
1107	            if max_sim > 0.92:
1108	                sent_lower = sent.lower()
1109	                has_marker = any(m in sent_lower for m in analytical_markers)
1110	                if not has_marker:
1111	                    copied += 1
1112	                    if len(examples) < 3:
1113	                        examples.append(sent[:100])
1114	
1115	        total = len(sentences)
1116	        return {
1117	            "total_sentences": total,
1118	            "original": total - copied,
1119	            "copied": copied,
1120	            "copy_ratio": round(copied / max(total, 1), 3),
1121	            "examples": examples,
1122	        }
1123	    except Exception as exc:
1124	        print(f"    Originality check failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {str(exc)[:100]}")
1125	        return {"total_sentences": len(sentences), "original": len(sentences),
1126	                "copied": 0, "copy_ratio": 0.0, "examples": []}

199	        "audit_bundle/test_conformance.py.\n"
200	    )
201	
202	
203	def _sign_manifest(fixture_dir: Path) -> None:
204	    """Sign manifest.yaml -> manifest.yaml.asc with the real POLARIS bundle key.
205	
206	    Fail-loud (LAW II): if POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is unset, or signing/verify fails,
207	    RAISE. This script must NEVER write a placeholder/stub signature — a stub was
208	    the root cause of the born-inconsistent fixture (I-ready-018 #1139): the
209	    committed fixtures ship a REAL armored signature, and a stub-writing regen
210	    would silently downgrade it. The conformance check only asserts the .asc is
211	    present + non-empty, but the bundle's whole point is a genuine signature, so
212	    every regeneration MUST produce one that `gpg --verify` accepts.
213	
214	    Key/passphrase come from POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID / POLARIS_GPG_PASSPHRASE, matching
215	    src/polaris_graph/audit_bundle/gpg_signer.load_config_from_env. Homedir: this
216	    script prefers POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR then GNUPGHOME (gpg_signer reads GNUPGHOME
217	    only); set POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR (as .env does) so both resolve the same keyring.
218	    """
219	    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip()
220	    if not key_id:
221	        raise RuntimeError(
222	            "POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is unset — cannot sign the canonical fixture. "
223	            "Set it (see .env / scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh) and re-run. "
224	            "Refusing to emit a placeholder stub (LAW II: no silent signature "
225	            "downgrade)."
226	        )
227	    manifest = fixture_dir / "manifest.yaml"
228	    signature = fixture_dir / "manifest.yaml.asc"
229	    if signature.exists():
230	        signature.unlink()
231	    homedir = (
232	        os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_HOMEDIR", "").strip()
233	        or os.environ.get("GNUPGHOME", "").strip()
234	    )
235	    passphrase = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_PASSPHRASE", "").strip()
236	    base = ["gpg", "--batch", "--yes", "--pinentry-mode", "loopback"]
237	    if homedir:
238	        base += ["--homedir", homedir]
239	    sign_cmd = list(base)
240	    if passphrase:
241	        sign_cmd += ["--passphrase", passphrase]
242	    # Deterministic signature (Codex #1139b P2): GPG embeds a creation time in the
243	    # detached sig, so a normal re-sign changes the .asc bytes even when content is
244	    # unchanged. Pin the creation time to the fixture's frozen FIXED_TIMESTAMP (with
245	    # a trailing '!' to freeze it, not advance) so re-running regen is byte-idempotent.
246	    faked_time = datetime.fromisoformat(FIXED_TIMESTAMP).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S") + "!"
247	    sign_cmd += ["--faked-system-time", faked_time]
248	    sign_cmd += [
249	        "--local-user", key_id, "--armor", "--detach-sign",
250	        "--output", str(signature), str(manifest),
251	    ]
252	    result = subprocess.run(sign_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
253	    if not signature.exists() or signature.stat().st_size == 0:
254	        raise RuntimeError(f"gpg signing failed: {result.stderr.strip()}")
255	    # LF-normalize so the committed signature is byte-stable cross-platform,
256	    # paired with the `tests/fixtures/signed_bundle/** -text` .gitattributes rule.
257	    signature.write_bytes(signature.read_bytes().replace(b"\r\n", b"\n"))
258	    verify = subprocess.run(
259	        base + ["--verify", str(signature), str(manifest)],
260	        capture_output=True, text=True,
261	    )
262	    if "Good signature" not in verify.stderr:
263	        raise RuntimeError(
264	            f"gpg --verify failed after signing {signature}: {verify.stderr.strip()}"
265	        )
266	    print(f"  signed manifest.yaml.asc with key {key_id} (gpg --verify: GOOD)")
267
