1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-deepfix-001 Wave 2e (#1344): rendered-report ACCEPTANCE HARNESS — the false-fired-pipeline reader.
3	
4	`REAL_PLAN_2026.md` names the biggest risk as the **false-fired pipeline**: the composition +
5	coverage flags turn ON, the writer-path logs look busy, but the rendered ``report.md`` is still
6	shallow or degraded (empty sections, disclosure-only bodies, glued chrome, no cross-source analysis).
7	Internal counters cannot see this — they report that the machinery ran, not that real prose shipped.
8	This harness is the reader that checks the **actual finished paragraphs**, not the counters.
9	
10	It is an ACCEPTANCE CHECK / TRIAGE, NOT a faithfulness gate. It reports per-check observations plus
11	an advisory top-level ``looks_false_fired`` heuristic. It NEVER raises, NEVER aborts, NEVER modifies
12	anything, and no hardcoded threshold decides anything critical — every threshold is an env-tunable
13	(LAW VI) used ONLY for the advisory flag. The process always exits 0 on content (argparse handles
14	``--help`` / bad-arg codes); a missing or malformed report/manifest yields a structured result with
15	``input_present: false``, never an exception.
16	
17	INDEPENDENT DETECTOR (I-wire-013 blind-predicate lesson,
18	``project_iwire013_blind_predicate_independent_detector_2026_06_26``): shared code = shared blind
19	spot. This harness imports ZERO production predicates — its chrome / truncation / analytical /
20	disclosure rules are authored independently here (own regexes, own heuristics), and the disclosure
21	label prefixes it recognizes are re-declared as literal constants (NOT imported) so it stays a
22	clean-room reader of the rendered text. Any divergence from a production predicate is a FEATURE (it
23	surfaces production blindness), never a bug to be resolved by unifying the two implementations.
24	
25	The six checks (each an observation, none a hard gate):
26	  1. WRITER PROSE SHIPPED per section          4. TWO-SIDED TREATMENT (debate sections)
27	  2. LABELED-FALLBACK-BLOCK RATE               5. CHROME / JUNK IN BODY (independent §-1.1 rules)
28	  3. ANALYTICAL-UNITS-IN-BODY                  6. RUBRIC-FACET COVERAGE PRESENCE (manifest, best-effort)
29	
30	Usage (LOCAL, offline, instant):
31	    python scripts/rendered_report_acceptance_harness.py --report outputs/<run>/report.md
32	    python scripts/rendered_report_acceptance_harness.py --report <r> --manifest <m> --json-out out.json
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import argparse
38	import json
39	import os
40	import re
41	import sys
42	from collections import Counter
43	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
44	from pathlib import Path
45	from typing import Any
46	
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	# LAW VI — thresholds are env-tunable and used ONLY for the advisory
49	# ``looks_false_fired`` flag. NONE of them gates / aborts / drops anything.
50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	_ENV_FALLBACK_RATE_MAX = "PG_ACCEPT_FALLBACK_RATE_MAX"
52	_ENV_MIN_PROSE_SECTION_FRAC = "PG_ACCEPT_MIN_PROSE_SECTION_FRAC"
53	_ENV_MIN_ANALYTICAL_UNITS = "PG_ACCEPT_MIN_ANALYTICAL_UNITS"
54	_ENV_CHROME_MAX = "PG_ACCEPT_CHROME_MAX"
55	_ENV_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR = "PG_ACCEPT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR"
56	
57	_DEFAULT_FALLBACK_RATE_MAX = 0.5
58	_DEFAULT_MIN_PROSE_SECTION_FRAC = 0.5
59	_DEFAULT_MIN_ANALYTICAL_UNITS = 1
60	_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX = 5
61	_DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR = 5
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class Thresholds:
66	    """Advisory-only knobs (LAW VI). Passed explicitly to :func:`analyze_report` so the analysis is
67	    deterministic and env-independent; :func:`main` builds one from env + CLI overrides."""
68	
69	    fallback_rate_max: float = _DEFAULT_FALLBACK_RATE_MAX
70	    min_prose_section_frac: float = _DEFAULT_MIN_PROSE_SECTION_FRAC
71	    min_analytical_units: int = _DEFAULT_MIN_ANALYTICAL_UNITS
72	    chrome_max: int = _DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX
73	    known_word_floor: int = _DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR
74	
75	
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	# Disclosure-label prefixes — RE-DECLARED as literals here (NOT imported from
78	# verified_compose) so the harness is a clean-room reader. These mirror
79	# verified_compose.py:1142-1151 exactly; if the production labels change, this
80	# harness intentionally keeps its own copy and any drift is a surfaced signal.
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	_DISCLOSURE_PREFIXES = (
83	    "[uncovered supporting evidence for:",
84	    "[verification incomplete:",
85	    "[insufficient verified evidence",
86	)
87	# The section-level curator-gap stub (a whole slot that failed strict verification). Matched as a
88	# containment signal, case-insensitive.
89	_CURATOR_GAP_RE = re.compile(
90	    r"did not survive strict verification|curator-actionable gap|this slot is a curator|"
91	    # N4 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): plain-English gap disclosure (carries [N]).
92	    r"insufficient verified evidence",
93	    re.IGNORECASE,
94	)
95	
96	# Section titles that are pipeline SCAFFOLDING, not carried-up source prose (excluded from the
97	# required-prose set). Matched case-insensitively against the header title's leading text.
98	_SCAFFOLDING_TITLES = (
99	    "reliability header",
100	    "methods",
101	    "capability disclosures",
102	    "contradiction disclosures",
103	    "evidence-support disclosure",
104	    "source corroboration",
105	    "bibliography",
106	    "references",
107	    "appendix",
108	    "disclosures",
109	    "research report:",  # the H1 echo of the question prompt — not a claim body
110	)
111	
112	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^(#{1,6})\s+(.*\S)\s*$")
113	_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
114	_RAW_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:")
115	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*[A-Za-z]|[A-Za-z]")
116	
117	# ---- Independent chrome rules (authored here; import NOTHING from production) --------------------
118	_ORCID_RE = re.compile(r"\b\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{3}[\dxX]\b")
119	_AFFIL_MIDDOT_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]{2,}\d{1,2}(?:,\d)?\s*[·•]")
120	_TOC_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|\s)\d+(?:\.\d+){1,3}\s+[A-Z][a-z]")
121	_INLINE_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"(?:^|[^\n#])#{1,6}\s+[A-Za-z]")
122	_NONLATIN_RE = re.compile(r"[؀-ۿݐ-ݿ一-鿿぀-ヿ가-힯]{4,}")
123	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://")
124	
125	# ---- Analytical connectives (independent lexicon) -----------------------------------------------
126	_ANALYTICAL_PATTERNS = (
127	    r"in contrast", r"by contrast", r"whereas", r"compared (?:to|with)", r"by comparison",
128	    r"however", r"conversely", r"on the other hand", r"relative to", r"more than", r"less than",
129	    r"higher than", r"lower than", r"greater than", r"as a result", r"because", r"therefore",
130	    r"thus", r"consequently", r"driven by", r"leads? to", r"gives? rise to", r"results? in",
131	    r"caused? by", r"counterbalanc", r"offset", r"outweigh", r"correlat", r"associated with",
132	    r"in line with", r"consistent with", r"at odds with", r"contradict", r"whilst",
133	)
134	_ANALYTICAL_RE = re.compile("|".join(_ANALYTICAL_PATTERNS), re.IGNORECASE)
135	
136	# ---- Two-sided debate detection (independent lexicon) -------------------------------------------
137	_DEBATE_TITLE_RE = re.compile(
138	    r"\bdebate\b|\bcontrovers|benefits? and risks?|opportunit\w* and challenge|"
139	    r"for and against|pros? and cons?|displacement .* creation|creation .* displacement|"
140	    r"two-sided|counter-?argument|competing",
141	    re.IGNORECASE,
142	)
143	_PRO_POLARITY_RE = re.compile(
144	    r"\bbenefit|\bimprove|\bincrease\w* (?:productivity|output|wages|employment|demand|jobs)|"
145	    r"\bcreat\w* (?:new )?jobs|\bgains?\b|\baugment|\bcomplement|\bopportunit|\bpositive|"
146	    r"\bgrowth\b|\bboost|\benhanc|\bhigher (?:wages|productivity|demand)",
147	    re.IGNORECASE,
148	)
149	_CON_POLARITY_RE = re.compile(
150	    r"\bdisplac|\bjob loss|\blos[et]\w* jobs|\breduc\w* (?:employment|wages|demand|jobs)|"
151	    r"\brisk\b|\bthreat|\bharm|\bnegative|\bunemploy|\binequalit|\bprecari|\bdecline\b|"
152	    r"\beliminat\w* (?:jobs|tasks|labor)|\bdisrupt",
153	    re.IGNORECASE,
154	)
155	
156	# Two-letter boundary tokens that are legitimate short words / abbreviations (never a span cut).
157	_SHORT_OK = frozenset(
158	    "ai it is of to in on or an as be by we us no so do al eg ie vs id ml ui ux hr ev uk eu io pp "
159	    "ed co re at if up my go he me ok".split()
160	)
161	# Suffixes that make a longer known word a mere INFLECTION of the token (so the token is the real
162	# base word, not a span cut): 'disadvantage' -> {'disadvantaged','disadvantages'} only -> NOT a cut.
163	_INFLECTIONS = ("s", "d", "es", "ed", "ing", "ly", "ic")
164	
165	
166	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167	# Section model + enumeration
168	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
169	@dataclass
170	class Section:
171	    title: str
172	    level: int
173	    body: str
174	    is_scaffolding: bool
175	
176	
177	def _is_scaffolding(title: str) -> bool:
178	    t = title.strip().lower().lstrip("# ").strip()
179	    return any(t.startswith(s) for s in _SCAFFOLDING_TITLES)
180	
181	
182	def split_sections(report_text: str) -> list[Section]:
183	    """Split the rendered report into ``Section`` records on markdown headers (any level). The
184	    pre-header preamble (if any) is captured as an untitled section and treated as scaffolding."""
185	    lines = str(report_text or "").split("\n")
186	    out: list[Section] = []
187	    cur_title, cur_level, cur_body = "", 0, []
188	    for ln in lines:
189	        m = _HEADER_RE.match(ln)
190	        if m:
191	            if cur_title or "".join(cur_body).strip():
192	                out.append(Section(cur_title, cur_level, "\n".join(cur_body),
193	                                    _is_scaffolding(cur_title) if cur_title else True))
194	            cur_title, cur_level, cur_body = m.group(2), len(m.group(1)), []
195	        else:
196	            cur_body.append(ln)
197	    if cur_title or "".join(cur_body).strip():
198	        out.append(Section(cur_title, cur_level, "\n".join(cur_body),
199	                            _is_scaffolding(cur_title) if cur_title else True))
200	    return out
201	
202	
203	def _strip_italic_disclaimer(body: str) -> str:
204	    """Drop leading ``_…_`` provenance-disclaimer paragraphs (pipeline boilerplate, not a claim)."""
205	    keep = []
206	    for ln in body.split("\n"):
207	        s = ln.strip()
208	        if s.startswith("_") and s.endswith("_") and len(s) > 2:
209	            continue
210	        keep.append(ln)
211	    return "\n".join(keep)

214	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
215	# Disclosure-block classification (Check 2 primitives)
216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	def is_disclosure_block(text: Any) -> bool:
218	    """True iff a paragraph / line IS a labeled-fallback disclosure block (one of the re-declared
219	    prefixes) OR a section-level curator-gap stub. Independent of production (literals re-declared)."""
220	    s = str(text or "").strip()
221	    if not s:
222	        return False
223	    low = s.lower()
224	    if any(low.startswith(p) for p in _DISCLOSURE_PREFIXES):
225	        return True
226	    # a whole short paragraph that is essentially the curator-gap stub
227	    if _CURATOR_GAP_RE.search(s) and len(s) <= 400:
228	        return True
229	    return False

232	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	# Sentence splitting (Check 1 + Check 3 primitive)
234	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	# A sentence boundary: terminal ``.!?`` then any trailing ``[N]`` citation markers (kept, attached to
236	# the LEFT sentence via the capture group) then whitespace then an uppercase / ``[`` sentence start.
237	# The captured citation run is required to follow real terminal punctuation, so ``Acemoglu[2] Restrepo``
238	# (no preceding ``.!?``) does NOT split. Decimals ('0.2') and lowercase continuations do not split.
239	_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])((?:\s*\[\d+\])*)\s+(?=[A-Z\[(])")
240	
241	
242	def _split_sentences(prose: str) -> list[str]:
243	    """Split body prose into candidate sentences (triage-grade, not linguistic). Trailing ``[N]``
244	    citation markers stay attached to the sentence they cite so per-sentence citation counts are
245	    correct."""
246	    text = " ".join(str(prose or "").split())
247	    if not text:
248	        return []
249	    # re.split with one capture group interleaves the captured citation runs: [s0, c0, s1, c1, ...].
250	    pieces = _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE.split(text)
251	    sentences: list[str] = []
252	    i = 0
253	    while i < len(pieces):
254	        sent = pieces[i] or ""
255	        cites = pieces[i + 1] if i + 1 < len(pieces) else ""
256	        combined = (sent + (cites or "")).strip()
257	        if combined:
258	            sentences.append(combined)
259	        i += 2
260	    return sentences
261	
262	
263	def _distinct_citation_ids(text: str) -> list[str]:
264	    seen: list[str] = []
265	    for m in _MARKER_RE.findall(text):
266	        if m not in seen:
267	            seen.append(m)
268	    return seen
269	
270	
271	def _looks_like_prose_sentence(sent: str) -> bool:
272	    """A real prose sentence: >= 6 alphabetic words and not itself a disclosure block. Filters the
273	    bare ``[6][7]`` citation-only fragments and one-word stubs that pad a degraded section."""
274	    if is_disclosure_block(sent):
275	        return False
276	    words = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]{2,}", sent)
277	    return len(words) >= 6

281	# Check 1 — writer prose shipped per section
282	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
283	def _iter_bullets(body: str) -> list[str]:
284	    out: list[str] = []
285	    cur: list[str] | None = None
286	    for ln in body.split("\n"):
287	        s = ln.strip()
288	        if s.startswith("- "):
289	            if cur is not None:
290	                out.append("\n".join(cur))
291	            cur = [s[2:]]
292	        elif cur is not None:
293	            cur.append(ln)
294	    if cur is not None:
295	        out.append("\n".join(cur))
296	    return out

299	def classify_section(section: Section) -> dict[str, Any]:
300	    """Classify one content section. Returns per-section observations incl. a ``verdict``:
301	    empty / disclosure_only / single_sentence / prose_shipped / bullets_present / bullets_degraded."""
302	    prose = _strip_italic_disclaimer(section.body)
303	    low_title = section.title.strip().lower()
304	    is_bulleted = low_title.startswith("key findings") or (
305	        len(_iter_bullets(prose)) >= 2 and len(_split_sentences(prose)) <= len(_iter_bullets(prose))
306	    )
307	
308	    # split body into paragraphs to count disclosure blocks vs real prose paragraphs
309	    paragraphs = [p.strip() for p in re.split(r"\n\s*\n", prose) if p.strip()]
310	    disclosure_paras = [p for p in paragraphs if is_disclosure_block(p)]
311	
312	    if is_bulleted:
313	        bullets = [b for b in _iter_bullets(prose) if b.strip()]
314	        real_bullets = [b for b in bullets if not is_disclosure_block(b) and re.search(r"[A-Za-z]{2,}", b)]
315	        verdict = "bullets_present" if real_bullets else ("bullets_degraded" if bullets else "empty")
316	        return {
317	            "title": section.title, "level": section.level, "type": "bulleted", "verdict": verdict,
318	            "n_bullets": len(bullets), "n_real_bullets": len(real_bullets),
319	            "n_sentences": 0, "n_disclosure_blocks": len(disclosure_paras),
320	        }
321	
322	    sentences = _split_sentences(prose)
323	    real_sentences = [s for s in sentences if _looks_like_prose_sentence(s)]
324	    n_real = len(real_sentences)
325	    if not prose.strip():
326	        verdict = "empty"
327	    elif n_real == 0 and (disclosure_paras or _CURATOR_GAP_RE.search(prose)):
328	        verdict = "disclosure_only"
329	    elif n_real == 0:
330	        verdict = "empty"
331	    elif n_real == 1:
332	        verdict = "single_sentence"
333	    else:
334	        verdict = "prose_shipped"
335	    return {
336	        "title": section.title, "level": section.level, "type": "prose", "verdict": verdict,
337	        "n_sentences": n_real, "n_disclosure_blocks": len(disclosure_paras), "n_bullets": 0,
338	        "n_real_bullets": 0,
339	    }

343	# Check 5 — independent chrome / junk detector (imports NO production predicate)
344	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345	def body_chrome_flags(text: str) -> list[str]:
346	    """Forensic chrome categories a body unit CONTAINS (containment rules, not whole-unit junk).
347	    Independent of production by construction. Returns the list of category labels that fire."""
348	    s = str(text or "")
349	    low = s.lower()
350	    flags: list[str] = []
351	
352	    if _RAW_EV_TOKEN_RE.search(s):
353	        flags.append("raw_ev_token")
354	
355	    if (
356	        "refresh the page or clear your browser cache" in low
357	        or "clear your browser cache" in low
358	        or "download associated records" in low
359	        or "most recent answer" in low
360	    ):
361	        flags.append("browser_ui")
362	
363	    if (
364	        "creative commons" in low
365	        or "creativecommons.org/licenses" in low
366	        or "open access article distributed under" in low
367	        or "this is an open access article" in low
368	        or re.search(r"©\s*20\d\d\s*the authors?", low)
369	    ):
370	        flags.append("license")
371	
372	    if (
373	        _ORCID_RE.search(s)
374	        or "orcid" in low
375	        or _AFFIL_MIDDOT_RE.search(s)
376	        or re.search(r"received:\s*\d", low)
377	        or re.search(r"accepted:\s*\d", low)
378	        or "published online" in low
379	    ):
380	        flags.append("author_meta")
381	
382	    if (
383	        re.search(r"\bdoi:\s*10\.\d", low)
384	        or re.search(r"\bissn\b\s*:?\s*\d", low)
385	        or "crossref reports the following articles citing" in low
386	        or "journal issn" in low
387	        or "volume title" in low
388	        or len(_URL_RE.findall(low)) >= 3
389	    ):
390	        flags.append("biblio_junk")
391	
392	    if _INLINE_HEADER_RE.search(s) or len(_TOC_TOKEN_RE.findall(s)) >= 2:
393	        flags.append("glued_header_toc")
394	
395	    if _NONLATIN_RE.search(s):
396	        flags.append("nonlatin_scrape")
397	
398	    return flags

401	# ---- truncation (own known-word basis; evaluated only when evidence_pool.json present) ----------
402	def build_known_words(snapshot_dir: Path | None, floor: int) -> tuple[set[str], int, bool]:
403	    """Every lowercase token occurring >= ``floor`` times across this run's fetched source text
404	    (``evidence_pool.json`` direct_quote/statement/title). Returns (known_set, source_chars,
405	    basis_available). A missing/unreadable evidence_pool.json => basis_available False (truncation
406	    then reported ``not_evaluated`` — never a silent pass, never a false flag)."""
407	    freq: Counter[str] = Counter()
408	    chars = 0
409	    if snapshot_dir is None:
410	        return set(), 0, False
411	    ev = Path(snapshot_dir) / "evidence_pool.json"
412	    if not ev.is_file():
413	        return set(), 0, False
414	    try:
415	        rows = json.loads(ev.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
416	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — malformed basis -> unavailable (never a false flag)
417	        return set(), 0, False
418	    for r in rows:
419	        if not isinstance(r, dict):
420	            continue
421	        for fkey in ("direct_quote", "statement", "title"):
422	            t = r.get(fkey) or ""
423	            if isinstance(t, str) and t:
424	                chars += len(t)
425	                for w in _WORD_RE.findall(t):
426	                    freq[w.lower()] += 1
427	    known = {w for w, c in freq.items() if c >= floor}
428	    return known, chars, True
429	
430	
431	def _has_longer_known_prefix(w: str, known: set[str]) -> bool:
432	    return any(len(k) > len(w) and k.startswith(w) and k[len(w):] not in _INFLECTIONS for k in known)
433	
434	
435	def _has_longer_known_suffix(w: str, known: set[str]) -> bool:
436	    return any(len(k) > len(w) and k.endswith(w) for k in known)
437	
438	
439	def _token_is_cut(token: str, known: set[str], *, mode: str) -> bool:
440	    if not token:
441	        return False
442	    t = token.lower()
443	    if t in known:
444	        return False
445	    completes = _has_longer_known_prefix(t, known) if mode == "end" else _has_longer_known_suffix(t, known)
446	    if len(t) == 1:
447	        return t not in {"a", "i"}
448	    if len(t) == 2:
449	        return t not in _SHORT_OK and completes
450	    return completes
451	
452	
453	def truncation_flags(body: str, known: set[str]) -> list[str]:
454	    """Mid-word span cuts at a ``[N]`` boundary in the body: the word immediately before a citation
455	    marker that is a non-inflectional prefix of a LONGER known corpus word ('Resea' -> 'research')."""
456	    if not known:
457	        return []
458	    out: list[str] = []
459	    # inspect the token right before each [N] marker
460	    for m in _MARKER_RE.finditer(str(body or "")):
461	        pre = body[max(0, m.start() - 40):m.start()]
462	        wm = re.search(r"([A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*)\.?\s*$", pre.rstrip())
463	        if not wm:
464	            continue
465	        w = wm.group(1).strip("-'")
466	        if _token_is_cut(w, known, mode="end"):
467	            out.append(f"end-cut:{w!r}")
468	    return out

474	def count_analytical_units(sections: list[Section]) -> dict[str, Any]:
475	    """Count body sentences that are cross-source analytical units (>=1 analytical connective AND
476	    >=2 DISTINCT [N] citations) vs plain single-source lookups (exactly 1 distinct citation, no
477	    analytical connective). Only non-scaffolding content sections are scanned."""
478	    analytical = 0
479	    single_source = 0
480	    examples: list[str] = []
481	    for sec in sections:
482	        if sec.is_scaffolding:
483	            continue
484	        if sec.title.strip().lower().startswith("key findings"):
485	            # bullets are handled as their own units
486	            units = [b for b in _iter_bullets(_strip_italic_disclaimer(sec.body)) if b.strip()]
487	        else:
488	            units = _split_sentences(_strip_italic_disclaimer(sec.body))
489	        for sent in units:
490	            if is_disclosure_block(sent):
491	                continue
492	            cites = _distinct_citation_ids(sent)
493	            has_analytical = bool(_ANALYTICAL_RE.search(sent))
494	            if has_analytical and len(cites) >= 2:
495	                analytical += 1
496	                if len(examples) < 8:
497	                    examples.append(" ".join(sent.split())[:200])
498	            elif len(cites) == 1 and not has_analytical:
499	                single_source += 1
500	    return {

content.text: This is a dense, precise, well-designed file. Continue reading remaining lines.

content.content:
500	    return {
501	        "cross_source_analytical_units": analytical,
502	        "single_source_lookups": single_source,
503	        "examples": examples,
504	    }

510	def two_sided_analysis(sections: list[Section], question: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
511	    """Detect debate framing (question / any content-section title) and, if present, whether both a
512	    supported PRO (positive-polarity sentence WITH a citation) and a supported CON exist in the body."""
513	    titles = " ".join(s.title for s in sections)
514	    debate = bool(_DEBATE_TITLE_RE.search(f"{question}\n{titles}"))
515	    pro = 0
516	    con = 0
517	    for sec in sections:
518	        if sec.is_scaffolding:
519	            continue
520	        body_units = _split_sentences(_strip_italic_disclaimer(sec.body))
521	        body_units += [b for b in _iter_bullets(_strip_italic_disclaimer(sec.body)) if b.strip()]
522	        for sent in body_units:
523	            if is_disclosure_block(sent):
524	                continue
525	            if not _distinct_citation_ids(sent):
526	                continue
527	            if _PRO_POLARITY_RE.search(sent):
528	                pro += 1
529	            if _CON_POLARITY_RE.search(sent):
530	                con += 1
531	    two_sided: bool | None
532	    missing: str | None
533	    if not debate:
534	        two_sided, missing = None, None
535	    else:
536	        two_sided = pro > 0 and con > 0
537	        missing = None if two_sided else ("con" if pro > 0 else ("pro" if con > 0 else "both"))
538	    return {
539	        "debate_detected": debate,
540	        "supported_pro_count": pro,
541	        "supported_con_count": con,
542	        "two_sided": two_sided,
543	        "missing_side": missing,
544	    }

550	_FACET_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
551	    "the a an and or of to in on at for with by from as is are was were be vs and rct trial study "
552	    "pivotal id type".split()
553	)

556	def _facet_keywords(entity_id: str) -> list[str]:
557	    toks = [t for t in re.split(r"[_\W]+", str(entity_id or "").lower()) if t]
558	    return [t for t in toks if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _FACET_STOPWORDS]

561	def _extract_facets(manifest: dict | None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
562	    """Best-effort facet/rubric list from a few known manifest schemas. Never raises."""
563	    if not isinstance(manifest, dict):
564	        return []
565	    facets: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
566	    seen: set[str] = set()
567	
568	    def _add(fid: str) -> None:
569	        fid = str(fid or "").strip()
570	        if fid and fid not in seen:
571	            seen.add(fid)
572	            facets.append({"facet_id": fid, "keywords": _facet_keywords(fid)})
573	
574	    fcr = manifest.get("frame_coverage_report")
575	    if isinstance(fcr, dict):
576	        for e in fcr.get("entries") or []:
577	            if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("entity_id"):
578	                _add(e["entity_id"])
579	    comp = manifest.get("completeness")
580	    if isinstance(comp, dict):
581	        for tid in comp.get("uncovered_topic_ids") or []:
582	            _add(tid)
583	        for tid in comp.get("covered_topic_ids") or []:
584	            _add(tid)
585	    # generic fallbacks
586	    for key in ("facets", "rubric", "required_facets", "expert_facets"):
587	        val = manifest.get(key)
588	        if isinstance(val, list):
589	            for item in val:
590	                if isinstance(item, str):
591	                    _add(item)
592	                elif isinstance(item, dict):
593	                    _add(item.get("facet_id") or item.get("id") or item.get("name") or "")
594	    return facets

597	def facet_coverage(body_text: str, manifest: dict | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
598	    facets = _extract_facets(manifest)
599	    if not facets:
600	        return {"facet_list_available": False, "facets": [], "present": 0, "absent": 0}
601	    low_body = str(body_text or "").lower()
602	    present: list[str] = []
603	    absent: list[str] = []
604	    detail: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
605	    for f in facets:
606	        fid = f["facet_id"]
607	        kws = f["keywords"]
608	        exact = fid.lower() in low_body
609	        hit = sum(1 for k in kws if k in low_body)
610	        is_present = exact or (bool(kws) and hit >= max(1, (len(kws) + 1) // 2))
611	        (present if is_present else absent).append(fid)
612	        detail.append({"facet_id": fid, "keywords_matched": hit, "keywords_total": len(kws),
613	                       "exact": exact, "present": is_present})
614	    return {
615	        "facet_list_available": True, "present": len(present), "absent": len(absent),
616	        "present_facets": present, "absent_facets": absent, "detail": detail,
617	    }

620	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
621	# Core analysis (pure, deterministic, env-independent)
622	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
623	def analyze_report(
624	    report_text: str,
625	    manifest: dict | None = None,
626	    snapshot_dir: Path | None = None,
627	    thresholds: Thresholds | None = None,
628	) -> dict[str, Any]:
629	    """Read the ACTUAL rendered report and return per-check observations + advisory
630	    ``looks_false_fired``. NEVER raises: any internal error degrades to a structured field."""
631	    th = thresholds or Thresholds()
632	    result: dict[str, Any] = {
633	        "input_present": bool(str(report_text or "").strip()),
634	        "thresholds": asdict(th),
635	    }
636	    try:
637	        question = ""
638	        if isinstance(manifest, dict):
639	            question = str(manifest.get("question") or manifest.get("research_question") or "")
640	
641	        sections = split_sections(report_text)
642	        content_sections = [s for s in sections if not s.is_scaffolding and s.title]
643	        if not question:
644	            for s in sections:
645	                if s.title.strip().lower().startswith("research report:"):
646	                    question = s.title.split(":", 1)[1].strip()
647	                    break
648	
649	        # ---- Check 1: writer prose shipped per section ----
650	        per_section = [classify_section(s) for s in content_sections]
651	        prose_secs = [c for c in per_section if c["type"] == "prose"]
652	        prose_shipped = [c for c in prose_secs if c["verdict"] == "prose_shipped"]
653	        prose_frac = (len(prose_shipped) / len(prose_secs)) if prose_secs else 0.0
654	        degraded = [c for c in per_section if c["verdict"] in ("empty", "disclosure_only", "bullets_degraded")]
655	        check1 = {
656	            "content_sections": len(content_sections),
657	            "prose_sections": len(prose_secs),
658	            "prose_shipped_sections": len(prose_shipped),
659	            "prose_shipped_fraction": round(prose_frac, 4),
660	            "degraded_sections": [c["title"] for c in degraded],
661	            "per_section": per_section,
662	        }
663	
664	        # ---- Check 2: labeled-fallback-block rate ----
665	        total_paras = 0
666	        disclosure_paras = 0
667	        disclosure_chars = 0
668	        total_chars = 0
669	        heavy_sections: list[str] = []
670	        for s in content_sections:
671	            prose = _strip_italic_disclaimer(s.body)
672	            paras = [p.strip() for p in re.split(r"\n\s*\n", prose) if p.strip()]
673	            sec_disc = [p for p in paras if is_disclosure_block(p)]
674	            total_paras += len(paras)
675	            disclosure_paras += len(sec_disc)
676	            for p in paras:
677	                total_chars += len(p)
678	            for p in sec_disc:
679	                disclosure_chars += len(p)
680	            if paras and len(sec_disc) / len(paras) >= 0.5:
681	                heavy_sections.append(s.title)
682	        unit_rate = (disclosure_paras / total_paras) if total_paras else 0.0
683	        char_rate = (disclosure_chars / total_chars) if total_chars else 0.0
684	        check2 = {
685	            "total_body_paragraphs": total_paras,
686	            "disclosure_paragraphs": disclosure_paras,
687	            "fallback_block_rate_by_unit": round(unit_rate, 4),
688	            "fallback_block_rate_by_char": round(char_rate, 4),
689	            "disclosure_heavy_sections": heavy_sections,
690	        }
691	
692	        # ---- Check 3: analytical units in body ----
693	        check3 = count_analytical_units(content_sections)
694	
695	        # ---- Check 4: two-sided treatment ----
696	        check4 = two_sided_analysis(content_sections, question)
697	
698	        # ---- Check 5: chrome / junk in body ----
699	        # Scan at the §-1.1 unit granularity: each body paragraph is split on ``[N]`` citation
700	        # markers into sub-units, so glued chrome inside a large blob (e.g. the Corroborated Weighted
701	        # Findings paragraph) is counted honestly, not as a single whole-paragraph hit.
702	        chrome_hits: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
703	        raw_ev_tokens = 0
704	        for s in content_sections:
705	            prose = _strip_italic_disclaimer(s.body)
706	            for para in [p.strip() for p in re.split(r"\n\s*\n", prose) if p.strip()]:
707	                if is_disclosure_block(para):
708	                    continue
709	                raw_ev_tokens += len(_RAW_EV_TOKEN_RE.findall(para))
710	                sub_units = [u.strip() for u in _MARKER_RE.split(para) if u.strip()]
711	                sub_hits = [(u, body_chrome_flags(u)) for u in sub_units]
712	                sub_hits = [(u, fl) for (u, fl) in sub_hits if fl]
713	                if sub_hits:
714	                    # fine-grained: one hit per chrome-flagged [N]-split sub-unit
715	                    for u, fl in sub_hits:
716	                        chrome_hits.append({"section": s.title, "flags": fl,
717	                                            "snippet": " ".join(u.split())[:160]})
718	                else:
719	                    # union floor: never undercount below the whole-paragraph signal
720	                    fl = body_chrome_flags(para)
721	                    if fl:
722	                        chrome_hits.append({"section": s.title, "flags": fl,
723	                                            "snippet": " ".join(para.split())[:160]})
724	        body_all = "\n".join(_strip_italic_disclaimer(s.body) for s in content_sections)
725	        known, known_chars, basis_ok = build_known_words(snapshot_dir, th.known_word_floor)
726	        trunc = truncation_flags(body_all, known) if basis_ok else []
727	        check5 = {
728	            "chrome_units": len(chrome_hits),
729	            "raw_ev_tokens": raw_ev_tokens,
730	            "chrome_examples": chrome_hits[:8],
731	            "truncation_evaluated": basis_ok,
732	            "truncation_count": len(trunc) if basis_ok else None,
733	            "truncation_examples": trunc[:8] if basis_ok else [],
734	            "known_word_basis_chars": known_chars,
735	        }
736	
737	        # ---- Check 6: rubric/facet coverage presence ----
738	        check6 = facet_coverage(body_all, manifest)
739	
740	        # ---- advisory looks_false_fired heuristic (env-tunable ONLY) ----
741	        reasons: list[str] = []
742	        if unit_rate > th.fallback_rate_max:
743	            reasons.append(f"fallback_block_rate {unit_rate:.2f} > {th.fallback_rate_max}")
744	        if prose_secs and prose_frac < th.min_prose_section_frac:
745	            reasons.append(f"prose_shipped_fraction {prose_frac:.2f} < {th.min_prose_section_frac}")
746	        if check3["cross_source_analytical_units"] < th.min_analytical_units:
747	            reasons.append(
748	                f"cross_source_analytical_units {check3['cross_source_analytical_units']} "
749	                f"< {th.min_analytical_units}"
750	            )
751	        if (len(chrome_hits) + raw_ev_tokens) > th.chrome_max:
752	            reasons.append(f"body_chrome {len(chrome_hits) + raw_ev_tokens} > {th.chrome_max}")
753	
754	        result.update({
755	            "question": question,
756	            "check1_writer_prose_shipped": check1,
757	            "check2_labeled_fallback_rate": check2,
758	            "check3_analytical_units": check3,
759	            "check4_two_sided": check4,
760	            "check5_chrome_junk": check5,
761	            "check6_facet_coverage": check6,
762	            "looks_false_fired": bool(reasons),
763	            "reasons": reasons,
764	        })
765	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — triage NEVER raises; degrade to a structured error field
766	        result["analysis_error"] = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
767	        result.setdefault("looks_false_fired", None)
768	        result.setdefault("reasons", [])
769	    return result

854	def _safe_print(text: str) -> None:
855	    """Print that can NEVER raise on a Windows cp1252 console when the text carries unicode (a real
856	    report body does). Falls back to an ascii-backslashreplace encode. Triage never crashes on I/O."""
857	    try:
858	        print(text)
859	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
860	        try:
861	            enc = (getattr(sys.stdout, "encoding", None) or "utf-8")
862	            sys.stdout.write(text.encode(enc, errors="backslashreplace").decode(enc, errors="replace") + "\n")
863	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — last resort: swallow, never propagate from a triage tool
864	            pass

889	        chrome_max=args.chrome_max

896	def _load_manifest(path: Path | None, report_dir: Path | None) -> dict | None:
897	    candidate = path
898	    if candidate is None and report_dir is not None:
899	        maybe = report_dir / "manifest.json"
900	        candidate = maybe if maybe.is_file() else None
901	    if candidate is None or not Path(candidate).is_file():
902	        return None
903	    try:
904	        data = json.loads(Path(candidate).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
905	        return data if isinstance(data, dict) else None
906	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — malformed manifest => treated as absent, never a raise

911	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
912	        description="I-deepfix-001 Wave 2e rendered-report ACCEPTANCE HARNESS (triage, never a gate)."
913	    )

915	                        help="rendered report.md to read (manifest/evidence_pool resolved from its dir)")
916	    parser.add_argument("--manifest", type=Path, default=None,
917	                        help="optional manifest.json (defaults to <report_dir>/manifest.json if present)")
918	    parser.add_argument("--json-out", type=Path, default=None, help="optional path to write the JSON result")
919	    parser.add_argument("--fallback-rate-max", type=float, default=None)
920	    parser.add_argument("--min-prose-section-frac", type=float, default=None)

922	    parser.add_argument("--chrome-max", type=int, default=None)

924	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

937	            report_dir = rp.resolve().parent

940	                  file=sys.stderr)
